See Pitfall #8 + `docs/runbook.md` for the full air-gap posture.\ne3b0c442  \n79b6b7a7  ## Planning Documents\ne3b0c442  \nb1e899f7  Always read these before substantive work — they define what we're building and why.\ne3b0c442  \n89b02b22  - `.planning/PROJECT.md` — project context, core value, key decisions\n71355551  - `.planning/REQUIREMENTS.md` — 68 v1 requirements (SERVE/PROFILE/HARNESS/TOOLS/CONTEXT/EVAL/TELEM/UX/REPRO; +TOOLS-10 web_search + UX-07 3-state badge in Phase 3.1) + v2 + Out-of-Scope\ne5aba753  - `.planning/ROADMAP.md` — 7-phase plan with success criteria and traceability\n1950354f  - `.planning/STATE.md` — current phase + project memory\na909fda3  - `.planning/research/SUMMARY.md` — research synthesis with phase recommendations\n88360b50  - `.planning/research/STACK.md` — pinned tech stack (vLLM, models, libraries, versions)\n95cc797c  - `.planning/research/FEATURES.md` — feature taxonomy (table-stakes / differentiators / anti-features)\n2d4106c8  - `.planning/research/ARCHITECTURE.md` — component boundaries, profile schema, deployment topology\n9dc8f308  - `.planning/research/PITFALLS.md` — 20 pitfalls (8 critical) with prevention strategies\na782144d  - `.planning/config.json` — workflow preferences (YOLO mode, Standard granularity, parallel execution, opus profile)\ne3b0c442  \nca9e478e  ## Pinned Tech Stack\ne3b0c442  \nefc69eab  - **Serving containers (per-family):** Qwen → `nvcr.io/nvidia/vllm:26.03.post1-py3` (NGC fastsafetensors-derived, ~3 min cold start). Gemma 4 → `vllm/vllm-openai:gemma4-0409-arm64-cu130` (upstream Day-1 image, vLLM 0.19.1.dev6 + Transformers 5.5.0, plain safetensors, ~8 min cold start). Never upstream PyPI wheels on Spark (SM121 kernel failures)\n88e10bce  - **Primary model (daily-driver):** `Qwen/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-FP8` (MoE, 3B active, decode p50 ~48 tok/s thermal-validated)\n03e20c20  - **Heavy-agent slot:** `Qwen/Qwen3-Coder-Next-80B-A3B-FP8` (RL-trained for tools, ~30–43 tok/s)\ne27942b2  - **Gemma MoE slot:** `google/gemma-4-26B-A4B-it` (4B active, decode p50 ~36 tok/s thermal-validated)\n42d76ec0  - **Phase 4.1 dense siblings (eval-only, opt-in via `/profile`, NOT daily-driver):** `Qwen/Qwen3.6-27B-FP8` (dense, decode p50 ~7.6 tok/s) + `google/gemma-4-31B-it` (dense, BF16→runtime FP8, decode p50 ~6.4 tok/s). Both bandwidth-bound by design on GB10 UMA — single-digit tok/s is **expected**, NOT a regression. Acceptance gate is thermal stability (preemptions=0, oom=0), NOT throughput. Each dense family ships two siblings: **`v1`** (KV-ceiling audit at gmu=0.86, ~110 GiB UMA reservation — preserved as bisection-result artifact) and **`v1.1`** (operational at gmu=0.55, the D-29-equivalent RAM-headroom retune; DEFAULT_VARIANT points here). `/profile qwen3.6-27b` and `/profile gemma-4-31b-it` resolve to v1.1 automatically. Phase 5 evaluates dense-vs-MoE on real coding tasks; until then, daily-driver default stays on Qwen 35B-A3B v3.1.\n8b96b3b1  - **Hardware-level KV ceiling:** all 4 profiles' KV bisection (Qwen 35B MoE v3, Qwen 27B dense v1, Gemma 26B MoE v2, Gemma 31B dense v1) converge to `gpu_memory_utilization=0.86` via `scripts/find_kv_budget.py` on this GB10 / 128 GB UMA box. The ceiling is the box, not the model. v3.1/v1.1 then back off to 0.55 for system-RAM headroom (Pitfall #3 trumps Pitfall #1 for sibling versions that re-target operator comfort, not no-preempt ceiling).\na3d22a70  - **Quantization:** FP8 only. NVFP4 is *slower* than FP16 on GB10 UMA (-23.6% at 32K context); ModelOpt 0.42.0 has a NaN bug.\n9a0362d1  - **Boot:** `VLLM_LOAD_FORMAT=fastsafetensors` (~3× cold-start speedup, proven in prior repo)\n96075533  - **Telemetry off:** `VLLM_NO_USAGE_STATS=1` always; air-gap test gates every release\n9c73a6f2  - **Harness substrate:** `@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent` v0.68.0 (pi-mono by Mario Zechner) — TypeScript/Node, MIT\nd71bd8b0  - **Structured output:** XGrammar (vLLM 0.19 default) — 3.5–100× faster than Outlines\na5d69c35  - **Speculative decoding:** Qwen3-MTP for Qwen models, EAGLE-3 (RedHatAI speculators) for Gemma\n1f63970d  - **Observability:** Self-hosted Langfuse v3 + OTel GenAI semconv (Docker Compose locally)\n19aade72  - **Eval:** terminal-bench 2.0 (primary) + SWE-bench Verified (milestone) + LiveCodeBench (rolling, contamination-resistant) + prior repo's Phase 1 prompts (continuity)\ne3b0c442  \n314b6272  ## Keystone Abstraction: Versioned Model Profiles\ne3b0c442  \n9a43a7f9  Profiles are the **only shared contract** between serving and harness. Treat them as load-bearing.\ne3b0c442  \nf1b90184  ```\n690110d4  profiles/<name>/v<N>/\n0757e204    serving.yaml        # vLLM engine args, sampling defaults, spec config — restart for engine fields\n20d96211    harness.yaml        # prompts, tool format, per-tool sampling, retry, compaction — hot-reloadable\n455df46f    prompts/            # system prompt + edit examples + tool descriptions (.md)\nf29c64e5    tool_schemas/       # JSON schemas in the format the model parses best\n91cdfbf1    grammars/           # XGrammar .lark files\ncd7fbdd6    PROFILE_NOTES.md    # provenance for every default (\"source: Qwen team blog 2026-04-16\")\nf1b90184  ```\ne3b0c442  \nc309eba1  **Rules:**\n3a388de9  - Profiles are immutable. **Behavioral changes** (modify, replace, or remove an existing key value, or change a prompt's bytes, or move a default) → new version directory. **Strictly additive changes** (add an entirely new top-level block whose absence already validates as `None`, leaving every pre-existing key byte-identical) → recompute the bundle hash in place, do NOT cut a new version. Examples of in-place: 04.4-03 added `context.memory:`; 04.5-02 added `subagents:`. Both modify zero pre-existing bytes; eval-replay against the prior version-string remains semantically valid because absent → None handlers exist throughout. The intent of the immutability rule is to prevent silent behavioral drift; an additive block whose absence is already a valid configuration carries no drift risk. **When in doubt, cut a new version** — the failure mode of an unnecessary version cut is paperwork; the failure mode of an in-place behavioral edit is a corrupted observability/eval history.\n2ded5773  - Every observability event and every benchmark result embeds `{profile.id, profile.version, profile.hash}`.\n010b8202  - **Anti-pattern:** model-shaped logic in code (e.g. `if \"qwen\" in name: use_hermes_parser`). All such logic lives in the profile.\ne3b0c442  \n18ccb5a3  ## Critical Pitfalls — Design Against From Day One\ne3b0c442  \n8ffeeb30  1. **\"More prompting\" trap** — prior repo's Qwen3 went 8.5→6.8 by adding rules. Always run the *full* eval suite before declaring any change positive. Subset tests hide regressions.\n44d6261b  2. **Silent system-prompt delivery failure** — vLLM `/v1/messages` and `/v1/chat/completions` handle system messages differently per chat template. Use `[SP_OK]` canary in every benchmark loop; log the assembled prompt hash.\n8dc27aef  3. **KV cache budget set from theory** — DGX Spark UMA shares model + KV + harness CPU. Default `gpu_memory_utilization=0.95` causes preemption. Start at 0.75, validate with 30-min sustained load.\na9823c31  4. **DGX Spark thermal throttle** — short benchmarks look fine; 2-hour sessions throttle 2.8→~2 GHz. Run a 2-hour sustained-load thermal validation per profile.\n091e22e1  5. **Hidden cloud dependencies** — `VLLM_NO_USAGE_STATS=1` required; HF gated models need auth even offline. Verify with explicit air-gap test. **Note (Phase 3.1):** SearxNG is NOT a hidden dep — it's the one loopback egress we intentionally ship and document. What makes it acceptable: self-hosted, digest-pinned, disable-able (`EMMY_WEB_SEARCH=off` or stop the container), observable (all queries logged to `tool.web_search` events). Air-gap CI split into two validators: `ci_verify_phase3` (STRICT — zero outbound, for inference-posture gate) and `ci_verify_research_egress` (PERMISSIVE — SearxNG OK, blocks 12 cloud-inference endpoints).\ne40425ad  6. **Grammar fighting the model** — constrained decoding is a correctness backstop, not a quality lever. Parse unconstrained first; retry under grammar on parse failure only. Include a no-grammar baseline in every profile.\n9773c986  7. **Speculative decoding regression** — only wins when draft is cheap, acceptance > 0.5, and workload isn't compute-saturated. Always run paired spec-on/spec-off benchmark.\na72485fb  8. **Test-set contamination** — HumanEval and original SWE-bench likely in 2026 pretraining. Use terminal-bench 2.0, LiveCodeBench, SWE-bench Verified. Include held-out and rephrased contamination controls.\ne3b0c442  \n26952f39  ## Design Principles\ne3b0c442  \n133698a9  - **Stand on shoulders, then experiment** — start from community best-practice settings (cite in `PROFILE_NOTES.md`); only run in-house experiments where consensus doesn't exist.\n6d846268  - **Two hard boundaries; the rest soft** — `harness ↔ emmy-serve` over loopback HTTP; both layers ↔ profile registry over disk. Everything else lives in one harness host process. Don't microservice a single-user tool.\n070964f3  - **Eval imports the harness as a library** — never bypass the harness in eval; you measure the agent, not the model.\nf6f8f936  - **Hash-anchored edits as default** — Hashline pattern (6.7→68.3% on 180 tasks for weak models). Plain string-replace only as fallback.\nf80a2655  - **MCP via pi extension, not a fork** — pi has a \"no MCP\" stance; emmy overrides via extension because MCP became LF-governed infrastructure in late 2025.\n9e008ac4  - **Bash-first tools** — pi's minimal floor (read/write/edit/bash + grep/find/ls + web_fetch + MCP). Building 20+ specialized tools burns context tokens for no benefit.\nc485015e  - **YOLO defaults + denylist** — pi-mono's correct insight: once an agent has read+write+bash, real isolation is impossible inside the loop. ~40% slowdown for false safety. Use git for undo.\n9c02fec2  - **URL config precedence: env > profile > literal default** — every endpoint URL the harness consumes (SearxNG, sidecar, vLLM base, Langfuse, MCP servers) MUST resolve in this order. Env wins so a wrapper can override per-machine without touching profile bytes (Phase 04.2's Mac client `EMMY_SERVE_URL` / `EMMY_SEARXNG_URL` / `EMMY_REMOTE_CLIENT` are the canonical case). Profile wins next so a self-hosted Spark deployment can pin a non-default URL without env gymnastics. Literal default is the D-33 LOCKED loopback (`127.0.0.1:<port>`) — never a public hostname. **Anti-pattern caught in Phase 04.2-followup:** session.ts read the profile URL eagerly and passed it explicitly into the runtime config, shadowing the env getter inside the tool module — env override silently no-op'd. Resolution helpers (e.g. `resolveSearxngBaseUrl(profileBaseUrl)` in `packages/emmy-ux/src/session.ts`) keep the precedence in one place; new URL config should follow the same pattern.\ne3b0c442  \n7136000e  ## Workflow\ne3b0c442  \nc8a204f1  This project uses GSD (`/gsd-*` slash commands):\ne3b0c442  \n00a79e5a  - `/gsd-progress` — show current state, route to next action\n00a2ceee  - `/gsd-plan-phase <N>` — decompose phase N into executable plans\n34b90012  - `/gsd-execute-phase <N>` — execute phase N's plans\n3e777142  - `/gsd-verify-work` — UAT after a phase completes\ne3b0c442  \n4d9a6833  Config (`.planning/config.json`):\n557db3f7  - Mode: **YOLO** (auto-approve)\n5faea7b8  - Granularity: **Standard** (5–8 phases, 3–5 plans each)\n2a393cbc  - Parallelization: **on**\n5302b2e1  - Model profile: **Quality** (opus for research/roadmap)\n8193587a  - Workflow agents: research **on**, plan-check **on**, verifier **on**\ne3b0c442  \na31d0680  ## Repo Layout (anticipated, will evolve)\ne3b0c442  \nf1b90184  ```\n8131a032  emmy/\n7ea8da3e  ├── CLAUDE.md                  # this file\nf5442314  ├── .planning/                 # GSD planning artifacts (committed)\n407704bf  ├── profiles/                  # versioned model profiles (the keystone)\n8b174088  │   └── qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v1/\nca4ed8d5  ├── emmy-serve/                # vLLM container wrapper, profile loader, hot-reload\n63e8fa03  ├── packages/                  # pi extensions (TypeScript)\na42dc9e7  │   ├── emmy-provider/         # local vLLM provider for pi\n44688873  │   ├── emmy-tools/            # hash-anchored edit, web_fetch, MCP bridge\n9625a337  │   ├── emmy-telemetry/        # OTel + Langfuse + lived-experience JSONL\naa6e2fea  │   └── emmy-ux/               # GPU/KV footer, offline-OK badge\n1ddf34d2  ├── eval/                      # benchmark suite (imports harness as library)\ne7ba1658  └── start_emmy.sh              # one-command boot\nf1b90184  ```\ne3b0c442  \n75061542  (Subject to phase planning — this is the working sketch from research.)\ne3b0c442  \n8c096e9f  ## What This Is Not\ne3b0c442  \n11ca3de2  - Not a hosted SaaS. Single-user, single-machine.\n8dd969a8  - Not a general assistant. Coding-only.\n9cbb9d9c  - Not a fine-tuning project. Stock weights only.\n536a50b6  - Not an IDE plugin. TUI-first.\n712786b7  - Not cloud-fallback-enabled. Zero cloud in the critical loop.\ne3b0c442  \ne7b78304  See `.planning/REQUIREMENTS.md` Out-of-Scope for the full anti-feature list with reasoning.\ne3b0c442  \ncb3f91d5  ---\n8afab1c0  *Updated 2026-04-20 after project initialization*\n","line_count":129},"isError":false,"timestamp":1777250161714}],"profile":{"id":"qwen3.6-35b-a3b","version":"v3.1","hash":"sha256:a06d1bea0f8662f753f7bf98d1bc0180d0b761d78e6a0872570b0af1cd6a3a2a","path":"/data/projects/emmy/profiles/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3.1"}}

Gated by per-profile allowlist PLUS recent-web_search-URL bypass.\n- web_search(query, max_results?): search the open web via local SearxNG at http://127.0.0.1:8888. Returns {title, url, snippet, engine}[] with upstream-engine fallback. Six commands: view, create, str_replace, insert, delete, rename. ./runs/phase4-kv/failed-first-attempt-probe-timeout/README.md:5:**Root cause:** `scripts/smoke_test.py` hardcoded `timeout_s=300` for `wait_for_vllm` — insufficient for Gemma 4 v2's plain-safetensors cold boot (~6–7 min). Fails before the bisection even starts its first drive iteration. The finder's `_restart_vllm` then misclassified the probe timeout as an OOM/preemption signal per its docstring ("creep past the 300s wait_for_vllm ceiling … functionally equivalent to a preemption failure"), wrote `gpu_memory_utilization=0.7` to `serving.yaml` with `iters=0`, and appended a bogus entry to `PROFILE_NOTES.md`.
./runs/phase4-kv/failed-first-attempt-probe-timeout/README.md:9:- `bisect-stdout.log` — finder stdout. Shows "BOOT REJECTED (wait_for_vllm): /v1/models did not respond in 300s" → "Finder complete: gpu_memory_utilization = 0.7 (ok_value=0.75, preempted_at=0.75, iters=0)"
./runs/phase4-kv/failed-first-attempt-probe-timeout/README.md:29:Revert of `profiles/gemma-4-26b-a4b-it/v2/serving.yaml` (0.7 → 0.55 seed) and `profiles/gemma-4-26b-a4b-it/v2/PROFILE_NOTES.md` (drop bogus `iters=0` entry) done via `git restore` before the re-run. Pitfall #1 discipline preserved: the finder remains the only writer to `gpu_memory_utilization` — a bad write from a misdiagnosed run was discarded, not hand-corrected.

./runs/phase4-kv/PENDING.md:9:`scripts/find_kv_budget.py` drives a real vLLM container boot loop against a real GPU. Each iteration boots the engine with a candidate `gpu_memory_utilization`, runs a 30-min sustained load against `/v1/completions`, polls `/metrics` for preemption events, and steps UP or HALTS. The orchestrator executing Phase 4-06 does not have DGX Spark GPU access — physical-rig-only script.
./runs/phase4-kv/PENDING.md:11:The Gemma 4 v1 bundle ships with a LOW-end seed `gpu_memory_utilization: 0.55` (Plan 04-01, per 04-RESEARCH §1 + Phase 3.1 UMA lesson). Before daily-driver use, the operator MUST bisect UP against the measured ceiling.
./runs/phase4-kv/PENDING.md:39:# 3. Finder writes measured value into serving.yaml:engine.gpu_memory_utilization.
./runs/phase4-kv/PENDING.md:40:#    Hand-edit PROFILE_NOTES.md frontmatter measured_values.gpu_memory_utilization to match,
./runs/phase4-kv/PENDING.md:69:If bisection halts at 0.55 (the seed) on **first boot** because preemption fires immediately, that's a surprising UMA result worth investigating before proceeding. File as `p4-kv-fail` blocker in STATE.md "TODOs / Blockers"; do NOT proceed to thermal replay (Task 2) — thermal inherits the gpu_memory_utilization value.

./.planning/phases/04.4-filesystem-memory-tool-append-only-prefix-compaction-polish-/runs/v3-rot-protection/session-2026-04-27T00-35-50-963Z.jsonl:25:{"ts":"2026-04-27T00:36:01.714Z","type":"message_end","message":{"role":"toolResult","toolCallId":"call_be9430d25cbd4ddd970d07b1","toolName":"read","content":[{"type":"text","text":"{\"path\":\"/data/projects/emmy/CLAUDE.md\",\"binary\":false,\"lines\":\"cd7dab24  # Emmy — CLAUDE.md\\ne3b0c442  \\nc56843da  > Fully local coding agent on NVIDIA DGX Spark. Two architecturally separate parts:\\n759f2f7b  > 1. **Specialized vLLM serving** for Gemma 4 + Qwen 3.6 (coding-tuned sampling, grammar-constrained tool output, long-context optimization, speculative decoding)\\ned882993  > 2. **pi.dev (`pi-mono`) based harness** exposing all 8 customization surfaces opinionated harnesses hide\\ne3b0c442  \\n98c29dc3  **Done bar:** daily-driver replacement for Claude Code AND research-grade reproducible artifact. **No cloud INFERENCE anywhere in the loop** — the LLM is 100% local, air-gap tested. **Local-first EGRESS** via a self-hosted SearxNG instance is the single authorized outbound component (loopback-bound from the harness's POV; disableable via `EMMY_WEB_SEARCH=off` for stricter posture). See Pitfall #8 + `docs/runbook.md` for the full air-gap posture.\\ne3b0c442  \\n79b6b7a7  ## Planning Documents\\ne3b0c442  \\nb1e899f7  Always read these before substantive work — they define what we're building and why.\\ne3b0c442  \\n89b02b22  - `.planning/PROJECT.md` — project context, core value, key decisions\\n71355551  - `.planning/REQUIREMENTS.md` — 68 v1 requirements (SERVE/PROFILE/HARNESS/TOOLS/CONTEXT/EVAL/TELEM/UX/REPRO; +TOOLS-10 web_search + UX-07 3-state badge in Phase 3.1) + v2 + Out-of-Scope\\ne5aba753  - `.planning/ROADMAP.md` — 7-phase plan with success criteria and traceability\\n1950354f  - `.planning/STATE.md` — current phase + project memory\\na909fda3  - `.planning/research/SUMMARY.md` — research synthesis with phase recommendations\\n88360b50  - `.planning/research/STACK.md` — pinned tech stack (vLLM, models, libraries, versions)\\n95cc797c  - `.planning/research/FEATURES.md` — feature taxonomy (table-stakes / differentiators / anti-features)\\n2d4106c8  - `.planning/research/ARCHITECTURE.md` — component boundaries, profile schema, deployment topology\\n9dc8f308  - `.planning/research/PITFALLS.md` — 20 pitfalls (8 critical) with prevention strategies\\na782144d  - `.planning/config.json` — workflow preferences (YOLO mode, Standard granularity, parallel execution, opus profile)\\ne3b0c442  \\nca9e478e  ## Pinned Tech Stack\\ne3b0c442  \\nefc69eab  - **Serving containers (per-family):** Qwen → `nvcr.io/nvidia/vllm:26.03.post1-py3` (NGC fastsafetensors-derived, ~3 min cold start). Gemma 4 → `vllm/vllm-openai:gemma4-0409-arm64-cu130` (upstream Day-1 image, vLLM 0.19.1.dev6 + Transformers 5.5.0, plain safetensors, ~8 min cold start). Never upstream PyPI wheels on Spark (SM121 kernel failures)\\n88e10bce  - **Primary model (daily-driver):** `Qwen/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-FP8` (MoE, 3B active, decode p50 ~48 tok/s thermal-validated)\\n03e20c20  - **Heavy-agent slot:** `Qwen/Qwen3-Coder-Next-80B-A3B-FP8` (RL-trained for tools, ~30–43 tok/s)\\ne27942b2  - **Gemma MoE slot:** `google/gemma-4-26B-A4B-it` (4B active, decode p50 ~36 tok/s thermal-validated)\\n42d76ec0  - **Phase 4.1 dense siblings (eval-only, opt-in via `/profile`, NOT daily-driver):** `Qwen/Qwen3.6-27B-FP8` (dense, decode p50 ~7.6 tok/s) + `google/gemma-4-31B-it` (dense, BF16→runtime FP8, decode p50 ~6.4 tok/s). Both bandwidth-bound by design on GB10 UMA — single-digit tok/s is **expected**, NOT a regression. Acceptance gate is thermal stability (preemptions=0, oom=0), NOT throughput. Each dense family ships two siblings: **`v1`** (KV-ceiling audit at gmu=0.86, ~110 GiB UMA reservation — preserved as bisection-result artifact) and **`v1.1`** (operational at gmu=0.55, the D-29-equivalent RAM-headroom retune; DEFAULT_VARIANT points here). `/profile qwen3.6-27b` and `/profile gemma-4-31b-it` resolve to v1.1 automatically. Phase 5 evaluates dense-vs-MoE on real coding tasks; until then, daily-driver default stays on Qwen 35B-A3B v3.1.\\n8b96b3b1  - **Hardware-level KV ceiling:** all 4 profiles' KV bisection (Qwen 35B MoE v3, Qwen 27B dense v1, Gemma 26B MoE v2, Gemma 31B dense v1) converge to `gpu_memory_utilization=0.86` via `scripts/find_kv_budget.py` on this GB10 / 128 GB UMA box. The ceiling is the box, not the model. v3.1/v1.1 then back off to 0.55 for system-RAM headroom (Pitfall #3 trumps Pitfall #1 for sibling versions that re-target operator comfort, not no-preempt ceiling).\\na3d22a70  - **Quantization:** FP8 only. NVFP4 is *slower* than FP16 on GB10 UMA (-23.6% at 32K context); ModelOpt 0.42.0 has a NaN bug.\\n9a0362d1  - **Boot:** `VLLM_LOAD_FORMAT=fastsafetensors` (~3× cold-start speedup, proven in prior repo)\\n96075533  - **Telemetry off:** `VLLM_NO_USAGE_STATS=1` always; air-gap test gates every release\\n9c73a6f2  - **Harness substrate:** `@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent` v0.68.0 (pi-mono by Mario Zechner) — TypeScript/Node, MIT\\nd71bd8b0  - **Structured output:** XGrammar (vLLM 0.19 default) — 3.5–100× faster than Outlines\\na5d69c35  - **Speculative decoding:** Qwen3-MTP for Qwen models, EAGLE-3 (RedHatAI speculators) for Gemma\\n1f63970d  - **Observability:** Self-hosted Langfuse v3 + OTel GenAI semconv (Docker Compose locally)\\n19aade72  - **Eval:** terminal-bench 2.0 (primary) + SWE-bench Verified (milestone) + LiveCodeBench (rolling, contamination-resistant) + prior repo's Phase 1 prompts (continuity)\\ne3b0c442  \\n314b6272  ## Keystone Abstraction: Versioned Model Profiles\\ne3b0c442  \\n9a43a7f9  Profiles are the **only shared contract** between serving and harness. Treat them as load-bearing.\\ne3b0c442  \\nf1b90184  ```\\n690110d4  profiles/<name>/v<N>/\\n0757e204    serving.yaml        # vLLM engine args, sampling defaults, spec config — restart for engine fields\\n20d96211    harness.yaml        # prompts, tool format, per-tool sampling, retry, compaction — hot-reloadable\\n455df46f    prompts/            # system prompt + edit examples + tool descriptions (.md)\\nf29c64e5    tool_schemas/       # JSON schemas in the format the model parses best\\n91cdfbf1    grammars/           # XGrammar .lark files\\ncd7fbdd6    PROFILE_NOTES.md    # provenance for every default (\\\"source: Qwen team blog 2026-04-16\\\")\\nf1b90184  ```\\ne3b0c442  \\nc309eba1  **Rules:**\\n3a388de9  - Profiles are immutable. **Behavioral changes** (modify, replace, or remove an existing key value, or change a prompt's bytes, or move a default) → new version directory. **Strictly additive changes** (add an entirely new top-level block whose absence already validates as `None`, leaving every pre-existing key byte-identical) → recompute the bundle hash in place, do NOT cut a new version. Examples of in-place: 04.4-03 added `context.memory:`; 04.5-02 added `subagents:`. Both modify zero pre-existing bytes; eval-replay against the prior version-string remains semantically valid because absent → None handlers exist throughout. The intent of the immutability rule is to prevent silent behavioral drift; an additive block whose absence is already a valid configuration carries no drift risk. **When in doubt, cut a new version** — the failure mode of an unnecessary version cut is paperwork; the failure mode of an in-place behavioral edit is a corrupted observability/eval history.\\n2ded5773  - Every observability event and every benchmark result embeds `{profile.id, profile.version, profile.hash}`.\\n010b8202  - **Anti-pattern:** model-shaped logic in code (e.g. `if \\\"qwen\\\" in name: use_hermes_parser`). All such logic lives in the profile.\\ne3b0c442  \\n18ccb5a3  ## Critical Pitfalls — Design Against From Day One\\ne3b0c442  \\n8ffeeb30  1. **\\\"More prompting\\\" trap** — prior repo's Qwen3 went 8.5→6.8 by adding rules. Always run the *full* eval suite before declaring any change positive. Subset tests hide regressions.\\n44d6261b  2. **Silent system-prompt delivery failure** — vLLM `/v1/messages` and `/v1/chat/completions` handle system messages differently per chat template. Use `[SP_OK]` canary in every benchmark loop; log the assembled prompt hash.\\n8dc27aef  3. **KV cache budget set from theory** — DGX Spark UMA shares model + KV + harness CPU. Default `gpu_memory_utilization=0.95` causes preemption. Start at 0.75, validate with 30-min sustained load.\\na9823c31  4. **DGX Spark thermal throttle** — short benchmarks look fine; 2-hour sessions throttle 2.8→~2 GHz. Run a 2-hour sustained-load thermal validation per profile.\\n091e22e1  5. **Hidden cloud dependencies** — `VLLM_NO_USAGE_STATS=1` required; HF gated models need auth even offline. Verify with explicit air-gap test. **Note (Phase 3.1):** SearxNG is NOT a hidden dep — it's the one loopback egress we intentionally ship and document. What makes it acceptable: self-hosted, digest-pinned, disable-able (`EMMY_WEB_SEARCH=off` or stop the container), observable (all queries logged to `tool.web_search` events). Air-gap CI split into two validators: `ci_verify_phase3` (STRICT — zero outbound, for inference-posture gate) and `ci_verify_research_egress` (PERMISSIVE — SearxNG OK, blocks 12 cloud-inference endpoints).\\ne40425ad  6. **Grammar fighting the model** — constrained decoding is a correctness backstop, not a quality lever. Parse unconstrained first; retry under grammar on parse failure only. Include a no-grammar baseline in every profile.\\n9773c986  7. **Speculative decoding regression** — only wins when draft is cheap, acceptance > 0.5, and workload isn't compute-saturated. Always run paired spec-on/spec-off benchmark.\\na72485fb  8. **Test-set contamination** — HumanEval and original SWE-bench likely in 2026 pretraining. Use terminal-bench 2.0, LiveCodeBench, SWE-bench Verified. Include held-out and rephrased contamination controls.\\ne3b0c442  \\n26952f39  ## Design Principles\\ne3b0c442  \\n133698a9  - **Stand on shoulders, then experiment** — start from community best-practice settings (cite in `PROFILE_NOTES.md`); only run in-house experiments where consensus doesn't exist.\\n6d846268  - **Two hard boundaries; the rest soft** — `harness ↔ emmy-serve` over loopback HTTP; both layers ↔ profile registry over disk. Everything else lives in one harness host process. Don't microservice a single-user tool.\\n070964f3  - **Eval imports the harness as a library** — never bypass the harness in eval; you measure the agent, not the model.\\nf6f8f936  - **Hash-anchored edits as default** — Hashline pattern (6.7→68.3% on 180 tasks for weak models). Plain string-replace only as fallback.\\nf80a2655  - **MCP via pi extension, not a fork** — pi has a \\\"no MCP\\\" stance; emmy overrides via extension because MCP became LF-governed infrastructure in late 2025.\\n9e008ac4  - **Bash-first tools** — pi's minimal floor (read/write/edit/bash + grep/find/ls + web_fetch + MCP). Building 20+ specialized tools burns context tokens for no benefit.\\nc485015e  - **YOLO defaults + denylist** — pi-mono's correct insight: once an agent has read+write+bash, real isolation is impossible inside the loop. ~40% slowdown for false safety. Use git for undo.\\n9c02fec2  - **URL config precedence: env > profile > literal default** — every endpoint URL the harness consumes (SearxNG, sidecar, vLLM base, Langfuse, MCP servers) MUST resolve in this order. Env wins so a wrapper can override per-machine without touching profile bytes (Phase 04.2's Mac client `EMMY_SERVE_URL` / `EMMY_SEARXNG_URL` / `EMMY_REMOTE_CLIENT` are the canonical case). Profile wins next so a self-hosted Spark deployment can pin a non-default URL without env gymnastics. Literal default is the D-33 LOCKED loopback (`127.0.0.1:<port>`) — never a public hostname. **Anti-pattern caught in Phase 04.2-followup:** session.ts read the profile URL eagerly and passed it explicitly into the runtime config, shadowing the env getter inside the tool module — env override silently no-op'd. Resolution helpers (e.g. `resolveSearxngBaseUrl(profileBaseUrl)` in `packages/emmy-ux/src/session.ts`) keep the precedence in one place; new URL config should follow the same pattern.\\ne3b0c442  \\n7136000e  ## Workflow\\ne3b0c442  \\nc8a204f1  This project uses GSD (`/gsd-*` slash commands):\\ne3b0c442  \\n00a79e5a  - `/gsd-progress` — show current state, route to next action\\n00a2ceee  - `/gsd-plan-phase <N>` — decompose phase N into executable plans\\n34b90012  - `/gsd-execute-phase <N>` — execute phase N's plans\\n3e777142  - `/gsd-verify-work` — UAT after a phase completes\\ne3b0c442  \\n4d9a6833  Config (`.planning/config.json`):\\n557db3f7  - Mode: **YOLO** (auto-approve)\\n5faea7b8  - Granularity: **Standard** (5–8 phases, 3–5 plans each)\\n2a393cbc  - Parallelization: **on**\\n5302b2e1  - Model profile: **Quality** (opus for research/roadmap)\\n8193587a  - Workflow agents: research **on**, plan-check **on**, verifier **on**\\ne3b0c442  \\na31d0680  ## Repo Layout (anticipated, will evolve)\\ne3b0c442  \\nf1b90184  ```\\n8131a032  emmy/\\n7ea8da3e  ├── CLAUDE.md                  # this file\\nf5442314  ├── .planning/                 # GSD planning artifacts (committed)\\n407704bf  ├── profiles/                  # versioned model profiles (the keystone)\\n8b174088  │   └── qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v1/\\nca4ed8d5  ├── emmy-serve/                # vLLM container wrapper, profile loader, hot-reload\\n63e8fa03  ├── packages/                  # pi extensions (TypeScript)\\na42dc9e7  │   ├── emmy-provider/         # local vLLM provider for pi\\n44688873  │   ├── emmy-tools/            # hash-anchored edit, web_fetch, MCP bridge\\n9625a337  │   ├── emmy-telemetry/        # OTel + Langfuse + lived-experience JSONL\\naa6e2fea  │   └── emmy-ux/               # GPU/KV footer, offline-OK badge\\n1ddf34d2  ├── eval/                      # benchmark suite (imports harness as library)\\ne7ba1658  └── start_emmy.sh              # one-command boot\\nf1b90184  ```\\ne3b0c442  \\n75061542  (Subject to phase planning — this is the working sketch from research.)\\ne3b0c442  \\n8c096e9f  ## What This Is Not\\ne3b0c442  \\n11ca3de2  - Not a hosted SaaS. Single-user, single-machine.\\n8dd969a8  - Not a general assistant. Coding-only.\\n9cbb9d9c  - Not a fine-tuning project. Stock weights only.\\n536a50b6  - Not an IDE plugin. TUI-first.\\n712786b7  - Not cloud-fallback-enabled. Zero cloud in the critical loop.\\ne3b0c442  \\ne7b78304  See `.planning/REQUIREMENTS.md` Out-of-Scope for the full anti-feature list with reasoning.\\ne3b0c442  \\ncb3f91d5  ---\\n8afab1c0  *Updated 2026-04-20 after project initialization*\\n\",\"line_count\":129}"}],"details":{"path":"/data/projects/emmy/CLAUDE.md","binary":false,"lines":"cd7dab24  # Emmy — CLAUDE.md\ne3b0c442  \nc56843da  > Fully local coding agent on NVIDIA DGX Spark. Two architecturally separate parts:\n759f2f7b  > 1. **Specialized vLLM serving** for Gemma 4 + Qwen 3.6 (coding-tuned sampling, grammar-constrained tool output, long-context optimization, speculative decoding)\ned882993  > 2. **pi.dev (`pi-mono`) based harness** exposing all 8 customization surfaces opinionated harnesses hide\ne3b0c442  \n98c29dc3  **Done bar:** daily-driver replacement for Claude Code AND research-grade reproducible artifact. **No cloud INFERENCE anywhere in the loop** — the LLM is 100% local, air-gap tested. **Local-first EGRESS** via a self-hosted SearxNG instance is the single authorized outbound component (loopback-bound from the harness's POV; disableable via `EMMY_WEB_SEARCH=off` for stricter posture). See Pitfall #8 + `docs/runbook.md` for the full air-gap posture.\ne3b0c442  \n79b6b7a7  ## Planning Documents\ne3b0c442  \nb1e899f7  Always read these before substantive work — they define what we're building and why.\ne3b0c442  \n89b02b22  - `.planning/PROJECT.md` — project context, core value, key decisions\n71355551  - `.planning/REQUIREMENTS.md` — 68 v1 requirements (SERVE/PROFILE/HARNESS/TOOLS/CONTEXT/EVAL/TELEM/UX/REPRO; +TOOLS-10 web_search + UX-07 3-state badge in Phase 3.1) + v2 + Out-of-Scope\ne5aba753  - `.planning/ROADMAP.md` — 7-phase plan with success criteria and traceability\n1950354f  - `.planning/STATE.md` — current phase + project memory\na909fda3  - `.planning/research/SUMMARY.md` — research synthesis with phase recommendations\n88360b50  - `.planning/research/STACK.md` — pinned tech stack (vLLM, models, libraries, versions)\n95cc797c  - `.planning/research/FEATURES.md` — feature taxonomy (table-stakes / differentiators / anti-features)\n2d4106c8  - `.planning/research/ARCHITECTURE.md` — component boundaries, profile schema, deployment topology\n9dc8f308  - `.planning/research/PITFALLS.md` — 20 pitfalls (8 critical) with prevention strategies\na782144d  - `.planning/config.json` — workflow preferences (YOLO mode, Standard granularity, parallel execution, opus profile)\ne3b0c442  \nca9e478e  ## Pinned Tech Stack\ne3b0c442  \nefc69eab  - **Serving containers (per-family):** Qwen → `nvcr.io/nvidia/vllm:26.03.post1-py3` (NGC fastsafetensors-derived, ~3 min cold start). Gemma 4 → `vllm/vllm-openai:gemma4-0409-arm64-cu130` (upstream Day-1 image, vLLM 0.19.1.dev6 + Transformers 5.5.0, plain safetensors, ~8 min cold start). Never upstream PyPI wheels on Spark (SM121 kernel failures)\n88e10bce  - **Primary model (daily-driver):** `Qwen/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-FP8` (MoE, 3B active, decode p50 ~48 tok/s thermal-validated)\n03e20c20  - **Heavy-agent slot:** `Qwen/Qwen3-Coder-Next-80B-A3B-FP8` (RL-trained for tools, ~30–43 tok/s)\ne27942b2  - **Gemma MoE slot:** `google/gemma-4-26B-A4B-it` (4B active, decode p50 ~36 tok/s thermal-validated)\n42d76ec0  - **Phase 4.1 dense siblings (eval-only, opt-in via `/profile`, NOT daily-driver):** `Qwen/Qwen3.6-27B-FP8` (dense, decode p50 ~7.6 tok/s) + `google/gemma-4-31B-it` (dense, BF16→runtime FP8, decode p50 ~6.4 tok/s). Both bandwidth-bound by design on GB10 UMA — single-digit tok/s is **expected**, NOT a regression. Acceptance gate is thermal stability (preemptions=0, oom=0), NOT throughput. Each dense family ships two siblings: **`v1`** (KV-ceiling audit at gmu=0.86, ~110 GiB UMA reservation — preserved as bisection-result artifact) and **`v1.1`** (operational at gmu=0.55, the D-29-equivalent RAM-headroom retune; DEFAULT_VARIANT points here). `/profile qwen3.6-27b` and `/profile gemma-4-31b-it` resolve to v1.1 automatically. Phase 5 evaluates dense-vs-MoE on real coding tasks; until then, daily-driver default stays on Qwen 35B-A3B v3.1.\n8b96b3b1  - **Hardware-level KV ceiling:** all 4 profiles' KV bisection (Qwen 35B MoE v3, Qwen 27B dense v1, Gemma 26B MoE v2, Gemma 31B dense v1) converge to `gpu_memory_utilization=0.86` via `scripts/find_kv_budget.py` on this GB10 / 128 GB UMA box. The ceiling is the box, not the model. v3.1/v1.1 then back off to 0.55 for system-RAM headroom (Pitfall #3 trumps Pitfall #1 for sibling versions that re-target operator comfort, not no-preempt ceiling).\na3d22a70  - **Quantization:** FP8 only. NVFP4 is *slower* than FP16 on GB10 UMA (-23.6% at 32K context); ModelOpt 0.42.0 has a NaN bug.\n9a0362d1  - **Boot:** `VLLM_LOAD_FORMAT=fastsafetensors` (~3× cold-start speedup, proven in prior repo)\n96075533  - **Telemetry off:** `VLLM_NO_USAGE_STATS=1` always; air-gap test gates every release\n9c73a6f2  - **Harness substrate:** `@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent` v0.68.0 (pi-mono by Mario Zechner) — TypeScript/Node, MIT\nd71bd8b0  - **Structured output:** XGrammar (vLLM 0.19 default) — 3.5–100× faster than Outlines\na5d69c35  - **Speculative decoding:** Qwen3-MTP for Qwen models, EAGLE-3 (RedHatAI speculators) for Gemma\n1f63970d  - **Observability:** Self-hosted Langfuse v3 + OTel GenAI semconv (Docker Compose locally)\n19aade72  - **Eval:** terminal-bench 2.0 (primary) + SWE-bench Verified (milestone) + LiveCodeBench (rolling, contamination-resistant) + prior repo's Phase 1 prompts (continuity)\ne3b0c442  \n314b6272  ## Keystone Abstraction: Versioned Model Profiles\ne3b0c442  \n9a43a7f9  Profiles are the **only shared contract** between serving and harness. Treat them as load-bearing.\ne3b0c442  \nf1b90184  ```\n690110d4  profiles/<name>/v<N>/\n0757e204    serving.yaml        # vLLM engine args, sampling defaults, spec config — restart for engine fields\n20d96211    harness.yaml        # prompts, tool format, per-tool sampling, retry, compaction — hot-reloadable\n455df46f    prompts/            # system prompt + edit examples + tool descriptions (.md)\nf29c64e5    tool_schemas/       # JSON schemas in the format the model parses best\n91cdfbf1    grammars/           # XGrammar .lark files\ncd7fbdd6    PROFILE_NOTES.md    # provenance for every default (\"source: Qwen team blog 2026-04-16\")\nf1b90184  ```\ne3b0c442  \nc309eba1  **Rules:**\n3a388de9  - Profiles are immutable. **Behavioral changes** (modify, replace, or remove an existing key value, or change a prompt's bytes, or move a default) → new version directory. **Strictly additive changes** (add an entirely new top-level block whose absence already validates as `None`, leaving every pre-existing key byte-identical) → recompute the bundle hash in place, do NOT cut a new version. Examples of in-place: 04.4-03 added `context.memory:`; 04.5-02 added `subagents:`. Both modify zero pre-existing bytes; eval-replay against the prior version-string remains semantically valid because absent → None handlers exist throughout. The intent of the immutability rule is to prevent silent behavioral drift; an additive block whose absence is already a valid configuration carries no drift risk. **When in doubt, cut a new version** — the failure mode of an unnecessary version cut is paperwork; the failure mode of an in-place behavioral edit is a corrupted observability/eval history.\n2ded5773  - Every observability event and every benchmark result embeds `{profile.id, profile.version, profile.hash}`.\n010b8202  - **Anti-pattern:** model-shaped logic in code (e.g. `if \"qwen\" in name: use_hermes_parser`). All such logic lives in the profile.\ne3b0c442  \n18ccb5a3  ## Critical Pitfalls — Design Against From Day One\ne3b0c442  \n8ffeeb30  1. **\"More prompting\" trap** — prior repo's Qwen3 went 8.5→6.8 by adding rules. Always run the *full* eval suite before declaring any change positive. Subset tests hide regressions.\n44d6261b  2. **Silent system-prompt delivery failure** — vLLM `/v1/messages` and `/v1/chat/completions` handle system messages differently per chat template. Use `[SP_OK]` canary in every benchmark loop; log the assembled prompt hash.\n8dc27aef  3. **KV cache budget set from theory** — DGX Spark UMA shares model + KV + harness CPU. Default `gpu_memory_utilization=0.95` causes preemption. Start at 0.75, validate with 30-min sustained load.\na9823c31  4. **DGX Spark thermal throttle** — short benchmarks look fine; 2-hour sessions throttle 2.8→~2 GHz. Run a 2-hour sustained-load thermal validation per profile.\n091e22e1  5. **Hidden cloud dependencies** — `VLLM_NO_USAGE_STATS=1` required; HF gated models need auth even offline. Verify with explicit air-gap test. **Note (Phase 3.1):** SearxNG is NOT a hidden dep — it's the one loopback egress we intentionally ship and document. What makes it acceptable: self-hosted, digest-pinned, disable-able (`EMMY_WEB_SEARCH=off` or stop the container), observable (all queries logged to `tool.web_search` events). Air-gap CI split into two validators: `ci_verify_phase3` (STRICT — zero outbound, for inference-posture gate) and `ci_verify_research_egress` (PERMISSIVE — SearxNG OK, blocks 12 cloud-inference endpoints).\ne40425ad  6. **Grammar fighting the model** — constrained decoding is a correctness backstop, not a quality lever. Parse unconstrained first; retry under grammar on parse failure only. Include a no-grammar baseline in every profile.\n9773c986  7. **Speculative decoding regression** — only wins when draft is cheap, acceptance > 0.5, and workload isn't compute-saturated. Always run paired spec-on/spec-off benchmark.\na72485fb  8. **Test-set contamination** — HumanEval and original SWE-bench likely in 2026 pretraining. Use terminal-bench 2.0, LiveCodeBench, SWE-bench Verified. Include held-out and rephrased contamination controls.\ne3b0c442  \n26952f39  ## Design Principles\ne3b0c442  \n133698a9  - **Stand on shoulders, then experiment** — start from community best-practice settings (cite in `PROFILE_NOTES.md`); only run in-house experiments where consensus doesn't exist.\n6d846268  - **Two hard boundaries; the rest soft** — `harness ↔ emmy-serve` over loopback HTTP; both layers ↔ profile registry over disk. Everything else lives in one harness host process. Don't microservice a single-user tool.\n070964f3  - **Eval imports the harness as a library** — never bypass the harness in eval; you measure the agent, not the model.\nf6f8f936  - **Hash-anchored edits as default** — Hashline pattern (6.7→68.3% on 180 tasks for weak models). Plain string-replace only as fallback.\nf80a2655  - **MCP via pi extension, not a fork** — pi has a \"no MCP\" stance; emmy overrides via extension because MCP became LF-governed infrastructure in late 2025.\n9e008ac4  - **Bash-first tools** — pi's minimal floor (read/write/edit/bash + grep/find/ls + web_fetch + MCP). Building 20+ specialized tools burns context tokens for no benefit.\nc485015e  - **YOLO defaults + denylist** — pi-mono's correct insight: once an agent has read+write+bash, real isolation is impossible inside the loop. ~40% slowdown for false safety. Use git for undo.\n9c02fec2  - **URL config precedence: env > profile > literal default** — every endpoint URL the harness consumes (SearxNG, sidecar, vLLM base, Langfuse, MCP servers) MUST resolve in this order. Env wins so a wrapper can override per-machine without touching profile bytes (Phase 04.2's Mac client `EMMY_SERVE_URL` / `EMMY_SEARXNG_URL` / `EMMY_REMOTE_CLIENT` are the canonical case). Profile wins next so a self-hosted Spark deployment can pin a non-default URL without env gymnastics. Literal default is the D-33 LOCKED loopback (`127.0.0.1:<port>`) — never a public hostname. **Anti-pattern caught in Phase 04.2-followup:** session.ts read the profile URL eagerly and passed it explicitly into the runtime config, shadowing the env getter inside the tool module — env override silently no-op'd. Resolution helpers (e.g. `resolveSearxngBaseUrl(profileBaseUrl)` in `packages/emmy-ux/src/session.ts`) keep the precedence in one place; new URL config should follow the same pattern.\ne3b0c442  \n7136000e  ## Workflow\ne3b0c442  \nc8a204f1  This project uses GSD (`/gsd-*` slash commands):\ne3b0c442  \n00a79e5a  - `/gsd-progress` — show current state, route to next action\n00a2ceee  - `/gsd-plan-phase <N>` — decompose phase N into executable plans\n34b90012  - `/gsd-execute-phase <N>` — execute phase N's plans\n3e777142  - `/gsd-verify-work` — UAT after a phase completes\ne3b0c442  \n4d9a6833  Config (`.planning/config.json`):\n557db3f7  - Mode: **YOLO** (auto-approve)\n5faea7b8  - Granularity: **Standard** (5–8 phases, 3–5 plans each)\n2a393cbc  - Parallelization: **on**\n5302b2e1  - Model profile: **Quality** (opus for research/roadmap)\n8193587a  - Workflow agents: research **on**, plan-check **on**, verifier **on**\ne3b0c442  \na31d0680  ## Repo Layout (anticipated, will evolve)\ne3b0c442  \nf1b90184  ```\n8131a032  emmy/\n7ea8da3e  ├── CLAUDE.md                  # this file\nf5442314  ├── .planning/                 # GSD planning artifacts (committed)\n407704bf  ├── profiles/                  # versioned model profiles (the keystone)\n8b174088  │   └── qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v1/\nca4ed8d5  ├── emmy-serve/                # vLLM container wrapper, profile loader, hot-reload\n63e8fa03  ├── packages/                  # pi extensions (TypeScript)\na42dc9e7  │   ├── emmy-provider/         # local vLLM provider for pi\n44688873  │   ├── emmy-tools/            # hash-anchored edit, web_fetch, MCP bridge\n9625a337  │   ├── emmy-telemetry/        # OTel + Langfuse + lived-experience JSONL\naa6e2fea  │   └── emmy-ux/               # GPU/KV footer, offline-OK badge\n1ddf34d2  ├── eval/                      # benchmark suite (imports harness as library)\ne7ba1658  └── start_emmy.sh              # one-command boot\nf1b90184  ```\ne3b0c442  \n75061542  (Subject to phase planning — this is the working sketch from research.)\ne3b0c442  \n8c096e9f  ## What This Is Not\ne3b0c442  \n11ca3de2  - Not a hosted SaaS. Single-user, single-machine.\n8dd969a8  - Not a general assistant. Coding-only.\n9cbb9d9c  - Not a fine-tuning project. Stock weights only.\n536a50b6  - Not an IDE plugin. TUI-first.\n712786b7  - Not cloud-fallback-enabled. Zero cloud in the critical loop.\ne3b0c442  \ne7b78304  See `.planning/REQUIREMENTS.md` Out-of-Scope for the full anti-feature list with reasoning.\ne3b0c442  \ncb3f91d5  ---\n8afab1c0  *Updated 2026-04-20 after project initialization*\n","line_count":129},"isError":false,"timestamp":1777250161714},"profile":{"id":"qwen3.6-35b-a3b","version":"v3.1","hash":"sha256:a06d1bea0f8662f753f7bf98d1bc0180d0b761d78e6a0872570b0af1cd6a3a2a","path":"/data/projects/emmy/profiles/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3.1"}}

All such logic lives in the profile.\\ne3b0c442  \\n18ccb5a3  ## Critical Pitfalls — Design Against From Day One\\ne3b0c442  \\n8ffeeb30  1. **\\\"More prompting\\\" trap** — prior repo's Qwen3 went 8.5→6.8 by adding rules. Always run the *full* eval suite before declaring any change positive. Subset tests hide regressions.\\n44d6261b  2. **Silent system-prompt delivery failure** — vLLM `/v1/messages` and `/v1/chat/completions` handle system messages differently per chat template. Use `[SP_OK]` canary in every benchmark loop; log the assembled prompt hash.\\n8dc27aef  3. **KV cache budget set from theory** — DGX Spark UMA shares model + KV + harness CPU. Default `gpu_memory_utilization=0.95` causes preemption. Start at 0.75, validate with 30-min sustained load.\\na9823c31  4. **DGX Spark thermal throttle** — short benchmarks look fine; 2-hour sessions throttle 2.8→~2 GHz. Run a 2-hour sustained-load thermal validation per profile.\\n091e22e1  5. **Hidden cloud dependencies** — `VLLM_NO_USAGE_STATS=1` required; HF gated models need auth even offline. Verify with explicit air-gap test. **Note (Phase 3.1):** SearxNG is NOT a hidden dep — it's the one loopback egress we intentionally ship and document. What makes it acceptable: self-hosted, digest-pinned, disable-able (`EMMY_WEB_SEARCH=off` or stop the container), observable (all queries logged to `tool.web_search` events). Air-gap CI split into two validators: `ci_verify_phase3` (STRICT — zero outbound, for inference-posture gate) and `ci_verify_research_egress` (PERMISSIVE — SearxNG OK, blocks 12 cloud-inference endpoints).\\ne40425ad  6. **Grammar fighting the model** — constrained decoding is a correctness backstop, not a quality lever. Parse unconstrained first; retry under grammar on parse failure only. Include a no-grammar baseline in every profile.\\n9773c986  7. **Speculative decoding regression** — only wins when draft is cheap, acceptance > 0.5, and workload isn't compute-saturated. Always run paired spec-on/spec-off benchmark.\\na72485fb  8. **Test-set contamination** — HumanEval and original SWE-bench likely in 2026 pretraining. Use terminal-bench 2.0, LiveCodeBench, SWE-bench Verified. Include held-out and rephrased contamination controls.\\ne3b0c442  \\n26952f39  ## Design Principles\\ne3b0c442  \\n133698a9  - **Stand on shoulders, then experiment** — start from community best-practice settings (cite in `PROFILE_NOTES.md`); only run in-house experiments where consensus doesn't exist.\\n6d846268  - **Two hard boundaries; the rest soft** — `harness ↔ emmy-serve` over loopback HTTP; both layers ↔ profile registry over disk. Everything else lives in one harness host process. Don't microservice a single-user tool.\\n070964f3  - **Eval imports the harness as a library** — never bypass the harness in eval; you measure the agent, not the model.\\nf6f8f936  - **Hash-anchored edits as default** — Hashline pattern (6.7→68.3% on 180 tasks for weak models). Plain string-replace only as fallback.\\nf80a2655  - **MCP via pi extension, not a fork** — pi has a \\\"no MCP\\\" stance; emmy overrides via extension because MCP became LF-governed infrastructure in late 2025.\\n9e008ac4  - **Bash-first tools** — pi's minimal floor (read/write/edit/bash + grep/find/ls + web_fetch + MCP). Building 20+ specialized tools burns context tokens for no benefit.\\nc485015e  - **YOLO defaults + denylist** — pi-mono's correct insight: once an agent has read+write+bash, real isolation is impossible inside the loop. ~40% slowdown for false safety. Use git for undo.\\n9c02fec2  - **URL config precedence: env > profile > literal default** — every endpoint URL the harness consumes (SearxNG, sidecar, vLLM base, Langfuse, MCP servers) MUST resolve in this order. Env wins so a wrapper can override per-machine without touching profile bytes (Phase 04.2's Mac client `EMMY_SERVE_URL` / `EMMY_SEARXNG_URL` / `EMMY_REMOTE_CLIENT` are the canonical case). Profile wins next so a self-hosted Spark deployment can pin a non-default URL without env gymnastics. Literal default is the D-33 LOCKED loopback (`127.0.0.1:<port>`) — never a public hostname. **Anti-pattern caught in Phase 04.2-followup:** session.ts read the profile URL eagerly and passed it explicitly into the runtime config, shadowing the env getter inside the tool module — env override silently no-op'd. Resolution helpers (e.g. `resolveSearxngBaseUrl(profileBaseUrl)` in `packages/emmy-ux/src/session.ts`) keep the precedence in one place; new URL config should follow the same pattern.\\ne3b0c442  \\n7136000e  ## Workflow\\ne3b0c442  \\nc8a204f1  This project uses GSD (`/gsd-*` slash commands):\\ne3b0c442  \\n00a79e5a  - `/gsd-progress` — show current state, route to next action\\n00a2ceee  - `/gsd-plan-phase <N>` — decompose phase N into executable plans\\n34b90012  - `/gsd-execute-phase <N>` — execute phase N's plans\\n3e777142  - `/gsd-verify-work` — UAT after a phase completes\\ne3b0c442  \\n4d9a6833  Config (`.planning/config.json`):\\n557db3f7  - Mode: **YOLO** (auto-approve)\\n5faea7b8  - Granularity: **Standard** (5–8 phases, 3–5 plans each)\\n2a393cbc  - Parallelization: **on**\\n5302b2e1  - Model profile: **Quality** (opus for research/roadmap)\\n8193587a  - Workflow agents: research **on**, plan-check **on**, verifier **on**\\ne3b0c442  \\na31d0680  ## Repo Layout (anticipated, will evolve)\\ne3b0c442  \\nf1b90184  ```\\n8131a032  emmy/\\n7ea8da3e  ├── CLAUDE.md                  # this file\\nf5442314  ├── .planning/                 # GSD planning artifacts (committed)\\n407704bf  ├── profiles/                  # versioned model profiles (the keystone)\\n8b174088  │   └── qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v1/\\nca4ed8d5  ├── emmy-serve/                # vLLM container wrapper, profile loader, hot-reload\\n63e8fa03  ├── packages/                  # pi extensions (TypeScript)\\na42dc9e7  │   ├── emmy-provider/         # local vLLM provider for pi\\n44688873  │   ├── emmy-tools/            # hash-anchored edit, web_fetch, MCP bridge\\n9625a337  │   ├── emmy-telemetry/        # OTel + Langfuse + lived-experience JSONL\\naa6e2fea  │   └── emmy-ux/               # GPU/KV footer, offline-OK badge\\n1ddf34d2  ├── eval/                      # benchmark suite (imports harness as library)\\ne7ba1658  └── start_emmy.sh              # one-command boot\\nf1b90184  ```\\ne3b0c442  \\n75061542  (Subject to phase planning — this is the working sketch from research.)\\ne3b0c442  \\n8c096e9f  ## What This Is Not\\ne3b0c442  \\n11ca3de2  - Not a hosted SaaS. Single-user, single-machine.\\n8dd969a8  - Not a general assistant. Coding-only.\\n9cbb9d9c  - Not a fine-tuning project. Stock weights only.\\n536a50b6  - Not an IDE plugin. TUI-first.\\n712786b7  - Not cloud-fallback-enabled. Zero cloud in the critical loop.\\ne3b0c442  \\ne7b78304  See `.planning/REQUIREMENTS.md` Out-of-Scope for the full anti-feature list with reasoning.\\ne3b0c442  \\ncb3f91d5  ---\\n8afab1c0  *Updated 2026-04-20 after project initialization*\\n\",\"line_count\":129}"}],"details":{"path":"/data/projects/emmy/CLAUDE.md","binary":false,"lines":"cd7dab24  # Emmy — CLAUDE.md\ne3b0c442  \nc56843da  > Fully local coding agent on NVIDIA DGX Spark. Two architecturally separate parts:\n759f2f7b  > 1. **Specialized vLLM serving** for Gemma 4 + Qwen 3.6 (coding-tuned sampling, grammar-constrained tool output, long-context optimization, speculative decoding)\ned882993  > 2. **pi.dev (`pi-mono`) based harness** exposing all 8 customization surfaces opinionated harnesses hide\ne3b0c442  \n98c29dc3  **Done bar:** daily-driver replacement for Claude Code AND research-grade reproducible artifact. **No cloud INFERENCE anywhere in the loop** — the LLM is 100% local, air-gap tested. **Local-first EGRESS** via a self-hosted SearxNG instance is the single authorized outbound component (loopback-bound from the harness's POV; disableable via `EMMY_WEB_SEARCH=off` for stricter posture). See Pitfall #8 + `docs/runbook.md` for the full air-gap posture.\ne3b0c442  \n79b6b7a7  ## Planning Documents\ne3b0c442  \nb1e899f7  Always read these before substantive work — they define what we're building and why.\ne3b0c442  \n89b02b22  - `.planning/PROJECT.md` — project context, core value, key decisions\n71355551  - `.planning/REQUIREMENTS.md` — 68 v1 requirements (SERVE/PROFILE/HARNESS/TOOLS/CONTEXT/EVAL/TELEM/UX/REPRO; +TOOLS-10 web_search + UX-07 3-state badge in Phase 3.1) + v2 + Out-of-Scope\ne5aba753  - `.planning/ROADMAP.md` — 7-phase plan with success criteria and traceability\n1950354f  - `.planning/STATE.md` — current phase + project memory\na909fda3  - `.planning/research/SUMMARY.md` — research synthesis with phase recommendations\n88360b50  - `.planning/research/STACK.md` — pinned tech stack (vLLM, models, libraries, versions)\n95cc797c  - `.planning/research/FEATURES.md` — feature taxonomy (table-stakes / differentiators / anti-features)\n2d4106c8  - `.planning/research/ARCHITECTURE.md` — component boundaries, profile schema, deployment topology\n9dc8f308  - `.planning/research/PITFALLS.md` — 20 pitfalls (8 critical) with prevention strategies\na782144d  - `.planning/config.json` — workflow preferences (YOLO mode, Standard granularity, parallel execution, opus profile)\ne3b0c442  \nca9e478e  ## Pinned Tech Stack\ne3b0c442  \nefc69eab  - **Serving containers (per-family):** Qwen → `nvcr.io/nvidia/vllm:26.03.post1-py3` (NGC fastsafetensors-derived, ~3 min cold start). Gemma 4 → `vllm/vllm-openai:gemma4-0409-arm64-cu130` (upstream Day-1 image, vLLM 0.19.1.dev6 + Transformers 5.5.0, plain safetensors, ~8 min cold start). Never upstream PyPI wheels on Spark (SM121 kernel failures)\n88e10bce  - **Primary model (daily-driver):** `Qwen/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-FP8` (MoE, 3B active, decode p50 ~48 tok/s thermal-validated)\n03e20c20  - **Heavy-agent slot:** `Qwen/Qwen3-Coder-Next-80B-A3B-FP8` (RL-trained for tools, ~30–43 tok/s)\ne27942b2  - **Gemma MoE slot:** `google/gemma-4-26B-A4B-it` (4B active, decode p50 ~36 tok/s thermal-validated)\n42d76ec0  - **Phase 4.1 dense siblings (eval-only, opt-in via `/profile`, NOT daily-driver):** `Qwen/Qwen3.6-27B-FP8` (dense, decode p50 ~7.6 tok/s) + `google/gemma-4-31B-it` (dense, BF16→runtime FP8, decode p50 ~6.4 tok/s). Both bandwidth-bound by design on GB10 UMA — single-digit tok/s is **expected**, NOT a regression. Acceptance gate is thermal stability (preemptions=0, oom=0), NOT throughput. Each dense family ships two siblings: **`v1`** (KV-ceiling audit at gmu=0.86, ~110 GiB UMA reservation — preserved as bisection-result artifact) and **`v1.1`** (operational at gmu=0.55, the D-29-equivalent RAM-headroom retune; DEFAULT_VARIANT points here). `/profile qwen3.6-27b` and `/profile gemma-4-31b-it` resolve to v1.1 automatically. Phase 5 evaluates dense-vs-MoE on real coding tasks; until then, daily-driver default stays on Qwen 35B-A3B v3.1.\n8b96b3b1  - **Hardware-level KV ceiling:** all 4 profiles' KV bisection (Qwen 35B MoE v3, Qwen 27B dense v1, Gemma 26B MoE v2, Gemma 31B dense v1) converge to `gpu_memory_utilization=0.86` via `scripts/find_kv_budget.py` on this GB10 / 128 GB UMA box. The ceiling is the box, not the model. v3.1/v1.1 then back off to 0.55 for system-RAM headroom (Pitfall #3 trumps Pitfall #1 for sibling versions that re-target operator comfort, not no-preempt ceiling).\na3d22a70  - **Quantization:** FP8 only. NVFP4 is *slower* than FP16 on GB10 UMA (-23.6% at 32K context); ModelOpt 0.42.0 has a NaN bug.\n9a0362d1  - **Boot:** `VLLM_LOAD_FORMAT=fastsafetensors` (~3× cold-start speedup, proven in prior repo)\n96075533  - **Telemetry off:** `VLLM_NO_USAGE_STATS=1` always; air-gap test gates every release\n9c73a6f2  - **Harness substrate:** `@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent` v0.68.0 (pi-mono by Mario Zechner) — TypeScript/Node, MIT\nd71bd8b0  - **Structured output:** XGrammar (vLLM 0.19 default) — 3.5–100× faster than Outlines\na5d69c35  - **Speculative decoding:** Qwen3-MTP for Qwen models, EAGLE-3 (RedHatAI speculators) for Gemma\n1f63970d  - **Observability:** Self-hosted Langfuse v3 + OTel GenAI semconv (Docker Compose locally)\n19aade72  - **Eval:** terminal-bench 2.0 (primary) + SWE-bench Verified (milestone) + LiveCodeBench (rolling, contamination-resistant) + prior repo's Phase 1 prompts (continuity)\ne3b0c442  \n314b6272  ## Keystone Abstraction: Versioned Model Profiles\ne3b0c442  \n9a43a7f9  Profiles are the **only shared contract** between serving and harness. Treat them as load-bearing.\ne3b0c442  \nf1b90184  ```\n690110d4  profiles/<name>/v<N>/\n0757e204    serving.yaml        # vLLM engine args, sampling defaults, spec config — restart for engine fields\n20d96211    harness.yaml        # prompts, tool format, per-tool sampling, retry, compaction — hot-reloadable\n455df46f    prompts/            # system prompt + edit examples + tool descriptions (.md)\nf29c64e5    tool_schemas/       # JSON schemas in the format the model parses best\n91cdfbf1    grammars/           # XGrammar .lark files\ncd7fbdd6    PROFILE_NOTES.md    # provenance for every default (\"source: Qwen team blog 2026-04-16\")\nf1b90184  ```\ne3b0c442  \nc309eba1  **Rules:**\n3a388de9  - Profiles are immutable. **Behavioral changes** (modify, replace, or remove an existing key value, or change a prompt's bytes, or move a default) → new version directory. **Strictly additive changes** (add an entirely new top-level block whose absence already validates as `None`, leaving every pre-existing key byte-identical) → recompute the bundle hash in place, do NOT cut a new version. Examples of in-place: 04.4-03 added `context.memory:`; 04.5-02 added `subagents:`. Both modify zero pre-existing bytes; eval-replay against the prior version-string remains semantically valid because absent → None handlers exist throughout. The intent of the immutability rule is to prevent silent behavioral drift; an additive block whose absence is already a valid configuration carries no drift risk. **When in doubt, cut a new version** — the failure mode of an unnecessary version cut is paperwork; the failure mode of an in-place behavioral edit is a corrupted observability/eval history.\n2ded5773  - Every observability event and every benchmark result embeds `{profile.id, profile.version, profile.hash}`.\n010b8202  - **Anti-pattern:** model-shaped logic in code (e.g. `if \"qwen\" in name: use_hermes_parser`). All such logic lives in the profile.\ne3b0c442  \n18ccb5a3  ## Critical Pitfalls — Design Against From Day One\ne3b0c442  \n8ffeeb30  1. **\"More prompting\" trap** — prior repo's Qwen3 went 8.5→6.8 by adding rules. Always run the *full* eval suite before declaring any change positive. Subset tests hide regressions.\n44d6261b  2. **Silent system-prompt delivery failure** — vLLM `/v1/messages` and `/v1/chat/completions` handle system messages differently per chat template. Use `[SP_OK]` canary in every benchmark loop; log the assembled prompt hash.\n8dc27aef  3. **KV cache budget set from theory** — DGX Spark UMA shares model + KV + harness CPU. Default `gpu_memory_utilization=0.95` causes preemption. Start at 0.75, validate with 30-min sustained load.\na9823c31  4. **DGX Spark thermal throttle** — short benchmarks look fine; 2-hour sessions throttle 2.8→~2 GHz. Run a 2-hour sustained-load thermal validation per profile.\n091e22e1  5. **Hidden cloud dependencies** — `VLLM_NO_USAGE_STATS=1` required; HF gated models need auth even offline. Verify with explicit air-gap test. **Note (Phase 3.1):** SearxNG is NOT a hidden dep — it's the one loopback egress we intentionally ship and document. What makes it acceptable: self-hosted, digest-pinned, disable-able (`EMMY_WEB_SEARCH=off` or stop the container), observable (all queries logged to `tool.web_search` events). Air-gap CI split into two validators: `ci_verify_phase3` (STRICT — zero outbound, for inference-posture gate) and `ci_verify_research_egress` (PERMISSIVE — SearxNG OK, blocks 12 cloud-inference endpoints).\ne40425ad  6. **Grammar fighting the model** — constrained decoding is a correctness backstop, not a quality lever. Parse unconstrained first; retry under grammar on parse failure only. Include a no-grammar baseline in every profile.\n9773c986  7. **Speculative decoding regression** — only wins when draft is cheap, acceptance > 0.5, and workload isn't compute-saturated. Always run paired spec-on/spec-off benchmark.\na72485fb  8. **Test-set contamination** — HumanEval and original SWE-bench likely in 2026 pretraining. Use terminal-bench 2.0, LiveCodeBench, SWE-bench Verified. Include held-out and rephrased contamination controls.\ne3b0c442  \n26952f39  ## Design Principles\ne3b0c442  \n133698a9  - **Stand on shoulders, then experiment** — start from community best-practice settings (cite in `PROFILE_NOTES.md`); only run in-house experiments where consensus doesn't exist.\n6d846268  - **Two hard boundaries; the rest soft** — `harness ↔ emmy-serve` over loopback HTTP; both layers ↔ profile registry over disk. Everything else lives in one harness host process. Don't microservice a single-user tool.\n070964f3  - **Eval imports the harness as a library** — never bypass the harness in eval; you measure the agent, not the model.\nf6f8f936  - **Hash-anchored edits as default** — Hashline pattern (6.7→68.3% on 180 tasks for weak models). Plain string-replace only as fallback.\nf80a2655  - **MCP via pi extension, not a fork** — pi has a \"no MCP\" stance; emmy overrides via extension because MCP became LF-governed infrastructure in late 2025.\n9e008ac4  - **Bash-first tools** — pi's minimal floor (read/write/edit/bash + grep/find/ls + web_fetch + MCP). Building 20+ specialized tools burns context tokens for no benefit.\nc485015e  - **YOLO defaults + denylist** — pi-mono's correct insight: once an agent has read+write+bash, real isolation is impossible inside the loop. ~40% slowdown for false safety. Use git for undo.\n9c02fec2  - **URL config precedence: env > profile > literal default** — every endpoint URL the harness consumes (SearxNG, sidecar, vLLM base, Langfuse, MCP servers) MUST resolve in this order. Env wins so a wrapper can override per-machine without touching profile bytes (Phase 04.2's Mac client `EMMY_SERVE_URL` / `EMMY_SEARXNG_URL` / `EMMY_REMOTE_CLIENT` are the canonical case). Profile wins next so a self-hosted Spark deployment can pin a non-default URL without env gymnastics. Literal default is the D-33 LOCKED loopback (`127.0.0.1:<port>`) — never a public hostname. **Anti-pattern caught in Phase 04.2-followup:** session.ts read the profile URL eagerly and passed it explicitly into the runtime config, shadowing the env getter inside the tool module — env override silently no-op'd. Resolution helpers (e.g. `resolveSearxngBaseUrl(profileBaseUrl)` in `packages/emmy-ux/src/session.ts`) keep the precedence in one place; new URL config should follow the same pattern.\ne3b0c442  \n7136000e  ## Workflow\ne3b0c442  \nc8a204f1  This project uses GSD (`/gsd-*` slash commands):\ne3b0c442  \n00a79e5a  - `/gsd-progress` — show current state, route to next action\n00a2ceee  - `/gsd-plan-phase <N>` — decompose phase N into executable plans\n34b90012  - `/gsd-execute-phase <N>` — execute phase N's plans\n3e777142  - `/gsd-verify-work` — UAT after a phase completes\ne3b0c442  \n4d9a6833  Config (`.planning/config.json`):\n557db3f7  - Mode: **YOLO** (auto-approve)\n5faea7b8  - Granularity: **Standard** (5–8 phases, 3–5 plans each)\n2a393cbc  - Parallelization: **on**\n5302b2e1  - Model profile: **Quality** (opus for research/roadmap)\n8193587a  - Workflow agents: research **on**, plan-check **on**, verifier **on**\ne3b0c442  \na31d0680  ## Repo Layout (anticipated, will evolve)\ne3b0c442  \nf1b90184  ```\n8131a032  emmy/\n7ea8da3e  ├── CLAUDE.md                  # this file\nf5442314  ├── .planning/                 # GSD planning artifacts (committed)\n407704bf  ├── profiles/                  # versioned model profiles (the keystone)\n8b174088  │   └── qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v1/\nca4ed8d5  ├── emmy-serve/                # vLLM container wrapper, profile loader, hot-reload\n63e8fa03  ├── packages/                  # pi extensions (TypeScript)\na42dc9e7  │   ├── emmy-provider/         # local vLLM provider for pi\n44688873  │   ├── emmy-tools/            # hash-anchored edit, web_fetch, MCP bridge\n9625a337  │   ├── emmy-telemetry/        # OTel + Langfuse + lived-experience JSONL\naa6e2fea  │   └── emmy-ux/               # GPU/KV footer, offline-OK badge\n1ddf34d2  ├── eval/                      # benchmark suite (imports harness as library)\ne7ba1658  └── start_emmy.sh              # one-command boot\nf1b90184  ```\ne3b0c442  \n75061542  (Subject to phase planning — this is the working sketch from research.)\ne3b0c442  \n8c096e9f  ## What This Is Not\ne3b0c442  \n11ca3de2  - ./.planning/phases/03.1-operational-polish-minimal-ram-profile-live-auto-compaction-/03.1-CONTEXT.md:12:**Fix A — RAM headroom (D-29):** `gpu_memory_utilization=0.88` on DGX Spark UMA (128 GB unified pool) reserves ~112 GB for GPU/KV, which steals from system RAM and produces 116/125 GB used + 8 GB swap during idle emmy-serve. CLAUDE.md Pitfall #3 explicitly warned ("Start at 0.75") but the Phase 1 KV-finder landed at 0.88 because the bisection objective was "highest value that doesn't preempt" rather than "highest value that leaves OS headroom". Drop to ~0.55 while KEEPING `max_model_len=131072` (user constraint: don't shrink context). Expected outcome: emmy-serve footprint ~50-70 GB, >40 GB system headroom, no swap pressure.

Output capped at ~40K chars (~10K tokens) with head+tail truncation — for research, prefer reading web_search snippets directly; only web_fetch when you need structured content a snippet can't convey. Gated by per-profile allowlist PLUS recent-web_search-URL bypass.\n- web_search(query, max_results?): search the open web via local SearxNG at http://127.0.0.1:8888. Returns {title, url, snippet, engine}[] with upstream-engine fallback. Use before web_fetch to look up current info / latest versions / docs.\n- memory(command, path, ...): read and write notes that persist across sessions. Two scopes via path prefix: /memories/project/... for repo-specific knowledge (project conventions, hard-won discoveries, dead-ends), /memories/global/... for cross-project preferences. Six commands: view, create, str_replace, insert, delete, rename. Check what's there with view /memories/project before non-trivial work; write notes ONLY when a discovery would help a future session — load-bearing facts (API quirks, conventions, counter-intuitive findings), not narrative.\n\nWhat's the v3.1 profile's gpu_memory_utilization value and why was it picked?","profile":{"id":"qwen3.6-35b-a3b","version":"v3.1","hash":"sha256:4da61fd4eec31ba079204ce43119ff39ced0ae0f1989037cc2a68c5a89c272ba","path":"/data/projects/emmy/profiles/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3.1"}}

./runs/phase4-kv/failed-first-attempt-probe-timeout/README.md:5:**Root cause:** `scripts/smoke_test.py` hardcoded `timeout_s=300` for `wait_for_vllm` — insufficient for Gemma 4 v2's plain-safetensors cold boot (~6–7 min). Fails before the bisection even starts its first drive iteration. The finder's `_restart_vllm` then misclassified the probe timeout as an OOM/preemption signal per its docstring ("creep past the 300s wait_for_vllm ceiling … functionally equivalent to a preemption failure"), wrote `gpu_memory_utilization=0.7` to `serving.yaml` with `iters=0`, and appended a bogus entry to `PROFILE_NOTES.md`.
./runs/phase4-kv/failed-first-attempt-probe-timeout/README.md:9:- `bisect-stdout.log` — finder stdout. Shows "BOOT REJECTED (wait_for_vllm): /v1/models did not respond in 300s" → "Finder complete: gpu_memory_utilization = 0.7 (ok_value=0.75, preempted_at=0.75, iters=0)"
./runs/phase4-kv/failed-first-attempt-probe-timeout/README.md:29:Revert of `profiles/gemma-4-26b-a4b-it/v2/serving.yaml` (0.7 → 0.55 seed) and `profiles/gemma-4-26b-a4b-it/v2/PROFILE_NOTES.md` (drop bogus `iters=0` entry) done via `git restore` before the re-run. Pitfall #1 discipline preserved: the finder remains the only writer to `gpu_memory_utilization` — a bad write from a misdiagnosed run was discarded, not hand-corrected.

./runs/phase4-kv/PENDING.md:9:`scripts/find_kv_budget.py` drives a real vLLM container boot loop against a real GPU. Each iteration boots the engine with a candidate `gpu_memory_utilization`, runs a 30-min sustained load against `/v1/completions`, polls `/metrics` for preemption events, and steps UP or HALTS. The orchestrator executing Phase 4-06 does not have DGX Spark GPU access — physical-rig-only script.
./runs/phase4-kv/PENDING.md:11:The Gemma 4 v1 bundle ships with a LOW-end seed `gpu_memory_utilization: 0.55` (Plan 04-01, per 04-RESEARCH §1 + Phase 3.1 UMA lesson). Before daily-driver use, the operator MUST bisect UP against the measured ceiling.
./runs/phase4-kv/PENDING.md:39:# 3. Finder writes measured value into serving.yaml:engine.gpu_memory_utilization.
./runs/phase4-kv/PENDING.md:40:#    Hand-edit PROFILE_NOTES.md frontmatter measured_values.gpu_memory_utilization to match,
./runs/phase4-kv/PENDING.md:69:If bisection halts at 0.55 (the seed) on **first boot** because preemption fires immediately, that's a surprising UMA result worth investigating before proceeding. File as `p4-kv-fail` blocker in STATE.md "TODOs / Blockers"; do NOT proceed to thermal replay (Task 2) — thermal inherits the gpu_memory_utilization value.
