acac5cfb  <code_context>
2976bc93  ## Existing Code Insights
e3b0c442  
b3f26250  - **`emmyCompactionTrigger` injection seam is already correct.** Plan 03-03 left `engine.summarize` as an injectable function that throws "not configured" by default. Plan 03.1-01 replaces that with the live wiring — NOT by implementing a raw HTTP round-trip, but by having the turn_start handler in `pi-emmy-extension.ts` call `ctx.compact({customInstructions})` DIRECTLY. This is a cleaner architecture than injecting a summarize function because pi's ctx.compact already knows about the active Model and apiKey. The `emmyCompactionTrigger` body keeps its role as the D-14 preservation classifier + D-11 soft-threshold + D-12 hard-ceiling guardrails; when the trigger determines compaction SHOULD fire, it now emits a structured directive (a preparation object) rather than calling summarize itself, and the turn_start handler reads the directive + calls ctx.compact. Net behavior: same soft-threshold semantics, same preservation semantics, now running against pi's native compaction engine via the active Model.
80918049  - **Slash command seam:** `pi.registerCommand("compact", ...)` + `pi.registerCommand("clear", ...)` inside `createEmmyExtension` — right next to the existing `pi.registerShortcut("shift+ctrl+up/down", ...)` (Plan 03-08 pattern). Both commands take `ExtensionCommandContext` which has `.compact()`, `.newSession()`, `.waitForIdle()`, `.abort()`.
8cdc5659  - **SearxNG JSON API is plain JSON over HTTP GET.** No auth headers, no rate-limiting from localhost, no websockets. Request shape: `GET /search?q=<urlencoded>&format=json&safesearch=0`. Response: `{query, results: [{title, url, content, engine, ...}], answers, suggestions, ...}`. The new `web_search` tool can be ~60 lines of TS (fetch + decode + map) — smaller than web_fetch.
fd74a8b9  - **Badge state-machine is already module-level** (Plan 03-06). `setInitialAudit`, `flipToViolation`, `bindBadge`, `_lastResult` — Plan 03.1-02 extends the enum from `{green, red}` to `{green, yellow, red}` and adds `flipToYellow(reason)` / `flipToGreen(reason)` for SearxNG up/down transitions. The existing `flipToViolation` stays reserved for the D-36 red state (non-loopback inference).
a3a95852  - **Profile hash `--write`** (Phase 2 Plan 02-07 pattern) — `uv run emmy profile hash --write profiles/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3.1` computes + writes to `profile.yaml`. Strips any `KNOWN-STALE` comments. v3 stays byte-identical (NOT modified).
a1a3c2b2  - **Four-way regression** (Phase 2+3 pattern): `bun test` + `bun run typecheck` + `uv run pytest tests/unit -q` + `uv run emmy profile validate v1+v2+v3+v3.1`. Any plan close that doesn't green all four is not a close. Plan 03.1-01 adds v3.1 to the validate sweep.
13f6e6ae  - **Air-gap CI split** (Plan 03.1-02): `emmy_serve/airgap/ci_verify_phase3.py` asserts ZERO outbound from emmy-serve + Langfuse. Plan 03.1-02 clones it to `ci_verify_research_egress.py` which permits `searxng.` outbound (container DNS name) but STILL blocks `api.openai.com`, `api.anthropic.com`, `*.cloudflare.com`, etc. Both validators live side-by-side; existing Phase 3 CI job continues to exist for the "strict loopback" gate while a new Phase 3.1 job covers "research egress permitted".
e3b0c442  
8d734aa3  </code_context>
e3b0c442  
ce630da1  <specifics>
5efbcb48  ## Specific Ideas
e3b0c442  
bf1820cf  - **Live-mode SC-2 3-variant matrix remains Phase 3 operator-gated (`p3-07 sc2 live green`).** Plan 03.1-01 resolves the wire-through blocker that was preventing live mode from even being attempted. After Plan 03.1-01 closes, the operator-gated item becomes UNBLOCKED but not CLOSED — closure still requires a ~2h GPU window. Plan 03.1-01 close walkthrough DOES include a live compaction firing (that's D-30's truth #7) but on a realistic single research session, not the full 200-turn synthetic SC-2 replay.
07d48375  - **SearxNG first-boot needs network.** Same as Langfuse + NGC vLLM. Plan 03.1-02 documents this: first pull of `searxng/searxng@sha256:...` requires connectivity. After that, `start_emmy.sh --airgap` is expected to work while SearxNG is running (because SearxNG's outbound connections go to Google/DDG/Brave/Bing UPSTREAMS — NOT to the SearxNG image registry).
334a6790  - **The "LOCAL LLM · WEB" badge wording matters.** Not "LOCAL LLM + WEB" (confusing — suggests web LLM); not "LOCAL INFERENCE · WEB SEARCH" (too long); not "LOCAL ONLY · SEARCH" (weak contrast). Middle-dot separator is the visual hint that inference is still local; web is an add-on.
93c27fdf  - **`/clear` is dangerous.** Unlike `/compact`, there's no undo. Plan 03.1-01 pins `ctx.ui.confirm("Clear session", "This will start a fresh session. Unsaved work in the transcript will remain on disk but the agent will lose all context. Continue?")` before `ctx.newSession()`. In non-interactive modes (`hasUI: false`), the command fails with a clear error message — `/clear` is interactive-only.
d8ce5a71  - **v3.1 is NOT v4.** The profile-version major bump is reserved for structural changes (new subsystem blocks, schema breaking changes). 0.88 → 0.55 is a tuning refinement. Adding `tools.web_search` block + `tools.web_fetch.search_bypass_ttl_ms` field is additive (schema stays backward-compat: Phase 3.1 pydantic patch declares them Optional, defaults to None/0). Hash changes → new version directory per D-02, but the minor-version naming preserves the "v3 → v3.1 → v4" intuition where v4 arrives with Gemma 4's arrival pattern change.
51042f58  - **Pitfall #3 is this phase's signature pitfall.** CLAUDE.md's exact quote: "Start at 0.75, validate with 30-min sustained load." Plan 03.1-01 effectively re-runs the Phase 1 KV-finder math with a stricter constraint: "highest value that doesn't preempt AND leaves ≥40GB system RAM free." The bisection isn't re-run; we set to 0.55 by conservative calculation and validate empirically with `free -h` + 10min warm session.
f2731592  - **Pitfall #6 still applies.** SP_OK canary fires on every session_start; `/clear` creates a new session; therefore `/clear` MUST NOT short-circuit the canary path. Plan 03.1-01 explicitly validates this in the walkthrough — after `/clear`, the boot banner should re-emit SP_OK within the first agent turn.
e3b0c442  
f6a17468  </specifics>
e3b0c442  
9ad1df08  <deferred>
99a3f2c5  ## Deferred Ideas (confirmed out-of-scope for Phase 3.1)
e3b0c442  
0550574a  - **Per-user SearxNG cookie settings** — SearxNG supports per-session UI preferences. Emmy is single-user and uses JSON API only; no cookies. Revisit only if we add a web UI.
c6999a1c  - **SearxNG engine weighting / category filters** — default engine priority (google > ddg > brave > bing) is hardcoded in settings.yml; emmy doesn't expose knobs. Revisit if certain search types (code, academic) need dedicated engines.
493dc0b7  - **Result scoring / de-duplication inside emmy** — SearxNG already de-duplicates cross-engine hits server-side. Emmy treats SearxNG's JSON output as authoritative.
10a56649  - **Cache warm-up / pre-fetch** — no; the agent's search pattern is unpredictable.
3f5170df  - **web_fetch bypass policy via domain bootstrap** — not needed; returned-URL bypass (D-35) covers the intended use case (agent searches, then fetches one of the returned results).
f7420c08  - **Manual `/search <query>` slash command** — redundant; the agent uses web_search as a tool call, and the user can ask the agent "search for X" via normal prompt.
7d8a0ef9  - **`/stop` or `/abort`** — pi 0.68 already handles Ctrl+C at the TUI layer; no need for a slash command duplicate.
aeeac4e7  - **Per-tool cost accounting** — lived-experience JSONL already captures `tool_calls` names; cost/metric rollup is Phase 5 eval territory.
0c901c50  - **`/profile v3` or `/profile v3.1` command** — Phase 4's `/profile` atomic-swap UX (UX-04) handles this. Phase 3.1 stays with "restart pi-emmy with a different `--profile` flag."
83f9568c  - **SearxNG WebUI for the operator** — port-bind 127.0.0.1:8888 makes the web UI reachable by browser for manual debug, but emmy doesn't document this as a supported path. Runbook mentions it briefly.
7e7dd96e  - **Compaction telemetry → Langfuse rich view** — existing `session.compaction.trigger/.complete/.fallback` events already flow through dual-sink to Langfuse; additional Langfuse-specific dashboards are Phase 7 polish.
e3b0c442  
5d8ff197  </deferred>
e3b0c442  
cb3f91d5  ---
e3b0c442

2abaf200  ## Phase Boundary

73a459dd  Phase 3.1 ships **three surgical fixes + one new capability + daily-ops docs**:
e3b0c442  
92d5012f  **Fix A — RAM headroom (D-29):** `gpu_memory_utilization=0.88` on DGX Spark UMA (128 GB unified pool) reserves ~112 GB for GPU/KV, which steals from system RAM and produces 116/125 GB used + 8 GB swap during idle emmy-serve. CLAUDE.md Pitfall #3 explicitly warned ("Start at 0.75") but the Phase 1 KV-finder landed at 0.88 because the bisection objective was "highest value that doesn't preempt" rather than "highest value that leaves OS headroom". Drop to ~0.55 while KEEPING `max_model_len=131072` (user constraint: don't shrink context). Expected outcome: emmy-serve footprint ~50-70 GB, >40 GB system headroom, no swap pressure.
e3b0c442  
30753602  **Fix B — Live compaction + manual escape (D-30, D-31, D-32):** Plan 03-03 shipped `emmyCompactionTrigger` on `pi.on("turn_start")` with D-14 preservation + D-16 fallback + D-12 fail-loud. SC-2 green in stub mode. But the trigger's `engine.summarize()` injection point was never wired to pi's native `ctx.compact(options)` method (verified at `dist/core/extensions/types.d.ts:224`). Result: agent hit the 114,688-token hard wall during a real research session; compaction never fired; no manual escape (`/clear` attempted as a pi slash command failed — `/clear` is not a pi 0.68 built-in, and at 114,689 tokens the session couldn't process any further input). Plan 03.1-01 wires the turn_start handler to call `ctx.compact({customInstructions})` directly and adds two pi slash commands: `/compact [optional instructions]` and `/clear`.
e3b0c442  
997f2de5  **Fix C — Local research egress (D-33, D-34, D-35, D-36):** v3 profile ships a 5-host doc allowlist (docs.python.org, developer.mozilla.org, docs.vllm.ai, huggingface.co, docs.langfuse.com). Too narrow for a daily-driver research loop. The thesis is "NO CLOUD INFERENCE" — the LLM always runs locally on emmy-serve. "NO OUTBOUND TRAFFIC AT ALL" was a broader interpretation that conflates the two. Phase 3.1 refines the thesis: local LLM is non-negotiable; local-first EGRESS (a single self-hosted SearxNG sibling to Langfuse) is acceptable for web research. SearxNG is self-hosted at `127.0.0.1:8888`, auditable, and the harness only talks to SearxNG (which in turn talks to Google + DDG + Brave + Bing upstreams). New `web_search` tool hits the loopback SearxNG. `web_fetch` gets returned-URL bypass semantics: any URL returned by `web_search` in the last N seconds/results is fetchable without allowlist entry. Badge semantics split into three states: `OFFLINE OK` (green) when SearxNG is down/disabled; `LOCAL LLM · WEB` (yellow) when SearxNG is up and web_search is live; `CLOUD INFERENCE` (red) reserved for actual thesis violation (non-loopback inference detected — never fires today).
e3b0c442  
6f0e448f  **New artifact — Profile v3.1 (D-37):** Sibling of v3 per D-02 immutability. v3 stays byte-identical. v3.1 = v3 + (a) tuned `serving.yaml` with `gpu_memory_utilization: 0.55`, `max_num_batched_tokens: 8192`; (b) updated `harness.yaml` with `tools.web_search` block + `tools.web_fetch` bypass policy (allowlist retained for backwards compat); (c) `PROFILE_NOTES.md` provenance for D-29..D-36; (d) new prompts nothing — compaction prompts unchanged from v3.
e3b0c442  
e6d6f8c8  **New docs (D-38 sequencing, Plan 03.1-03):** Top-level `README.md` for the project (prerequisites + quickstart + stack boot/teardown + profile switching + troubleshooting); `docs/runbook.md` for daily ops; `CLAUDE.md` revised to split "No cloud INFERENCE" (hard invariant, air-gap tested) from "Local-first EGRESS" (SearxNG is the only outbound component, auditable, loopback-only from harness's POV).
e3b0c442

94b8c472  ### D-29 — RAM target
d8de2c5c  Drop `serving.yaml.engine.gpu_memory_utilization` from 0.88 → 0.55 in v3.1. KEEP `max_model_len=131072` (user constraint: don't reduce model capability). Drop `max_num_batched_tokens` from 16384 → 8192 (still well-above the "interactive latency" threshold from STACK.md; reduces CUDA workspace footprint). Target emmy-serve footprint ~50-70 GB, leaving >40 GB system headroom. Post-deploy validation: `free -h` MUST show `available ≥ 40G` after 10 min of warm emmy-serve + Langfuse compose. v3 profile is NOT modified.
e3b0c442  
c1251dfa  ### D-30 — Live compaction actually fires
785f8d66  `emmyCompactionTrigger` at `packages/emmy-context/src/compaction.ts` already contains D-11 soft-threshold check, D-14 preservation pre-filter, D-12 hard-ceiling, D-16 fallback. The `pi-emmy-extension.ts` `pi.on("turn_start", ...)` handler invokes the trigger but today's `engine.summarize()` default throws ("not configured"). D-30 locks the live wiring: the turn_start handler MUST call `ctx.compact({customInstructions})` directly (pi 0.68 ExtensionContext.compact method, verified at `dist/core/extensions/types.d.ts:224`). Emmy retains its preservation classifier (runs BEFORE the ctx.compact call to annotate which entries are preservation-critical), but the actual summarization round-trip flows through pi's native compaction engine — which already reuses the active Model from the session (the same emmy-vllm endpoint). Preservation-policy enforcement happens by passing `customInstructions` that include emmy's D-14 preservation directive text (from `prompts/compact.md`).
e3b0c442  
50f0953f  ### D-31 — /compact slash command
161f0697  Add `pi.registerCommand("compact", { handler })` in `packages/emmy-ux/src/pi-emmy-extension.ts`. Handler signature: `async (args, ctx) => ctx.compact({ customInstructions: args || undefined })`. Manual trigger; user can force compaction at any moment (useful when running many file reads before asking for a summary). Works even below the D-11 soft threshold.
e3b0c442  
c9e527e5  ### D-32 — /clear slash command
567a7bd8  Add `pi.registerCommand("clear", { handler })` in `packages/emmy-ux/src/pi-emmy-extension.ts`. Handler uses `ctx.newSession({})` from pi 0.68 `ExtensionCommandContext` (verified at `dist/core/extensions/types.d.ts:236-241`). Emmy's SP_OK canary + 3-layer prompt assembly re-run automatically on the new session because session.ts's boot hooks fire on each AgentSession creation. `/clear` is the only escape from an over-full session state short of killing pi-emmy.
e3b0c442  
a67690dd  ### D-33 — SearxNG topology
dff64743  Self-hosted SearxNG Docker Compose stack at `observability/searxng/docker-compose.yaml`, sibling of `observability/langfuse/docker-compose.yaml`. Single service (plus Redis for the search cache — use the SAME `redis` container Langfuse already runs via `external: true`, no new Redis). Digest-pinned image per D-09 (Phase 3) air-gap discipline. Bind `127.0.0.1:8888:8080` loopback-only. Config `observability/searxng/settings.yml` enables these engines:
7fca3f4c  - `google` (primary, preferred)
18d5e699  - `duckduckgo`, `brave`, `bing` (automatic fallbacks — SearxNG rotates on rate-limit or timeout, which is the exact problem we're solving)
e3b0c442  
b2370b0e  Setup script `scripts/start_searxng.sh` mirrors `start_observability.sh`: openssl-generated secret-key, health-gate with 60s timeout, instructions banner at end. `scripts/stop_searxng.sh` mirrors `stop_observability.sh` with optional `--volumes` flag.
e3b0c442  
d60f9776  ### D-34 — web_search tool
4ed5fcd0  New tool at `packages/emmy-tools/src/web-search.ts`:
f1b90184  ```
ba52ea45  web_search(query: string, max_results?: number = 10): Promise<SearchResult[]>
f1b90184  ```
2ae75dd2  Where `SearchResult = { title: string; url: string; snippet: string; engine: string }`. Implementation: HTTP GET to `http://127.0.0.1:8888/search?q=<urlencoded>&format=json&safesearch=0&max_results=<n>`. On HTTP 5xx or timeout (10s default), returns empty array + emits `tool.web_search.fallback` telemetry event; does NOT throw (pi agent continues). On kill-switch (EMMY_WEB_SEARCH=off OR EMMY_TELEMETRY=off), the tool is unregistered — pi never advertises it to the model.
e3b0c442  
0d166580  ### D-35 — web_fetch returned-URL bypass
18539d8c  `packages/emmy-tools/src/web-fetch-allowlist.ts` grows a sibling concept to the hostname allowlist: a rolling in-memory `recentSearchUrls: Set<string>` populated by `web_search` on every successful call. URL is fetchable IFF `hostname ∈ allowlist` OR `url ∈ recentSearchUrls` (exact URL match, not hostname). Entries expire after 5 minutes (configurable via `tools.web_fetch.search_bypass_ttl_ms` in harness.yaml, default 300000). Plan 03.1-02 also preserves v3.1's hostname allowlist with the same 5 default hosts as v3, so existing allowlisted doc-host flows continue to work.

f682467e  - `[emmy] LOCAL LLM · WEB` (yellow, new ANSI 33) — SearxNG is up and healthy; web_search live; LLM inference still local-only
0be0c140  - `[emmy] CLOUD INFERENCE` (red) — reserved for actual thesis violation; non-loopback inference endpoint detected
e3b0c442  
bd122858  The existing "NETWORK USED" label is DEPRECATED; boot-time tool audit now distinguishes benign egress (SearxNG) from thesis-breaking egress (non-loopback vLLM/inference). 2976bc93  ## Existing Code Insights
e3b0c442  
b3f26250  - **`emmyCompactionTrigger` injection seam is already correct.** Plan 03-03 left `engine.summarize` as an injectable function that throws "not configured" by default. Plan 03.1-01 replaces that with the live wiring — NOT by implementing a raw HTTP round-trip, but by having the turn_start handler in `pi-emmy-extension.ts` call `ctx.compact({customInstructions})` DIRECTLY. This is a cleaner architecture than injecting a summarize function because pi's ctx.compact already knows about the active Model and apiKey. The `emmyCompactionTrigger` body keeps its role as the D-14 preservation classifier + D-11 soft-threshold + D-12 hard-ceiling guardrails; when the trigger determines compaction SHOULD fire, it now emits a structured directive (a preparation object) rather than calling summarize itself, and the turn_start handler reads the directive + calls ctx.compact. Net behavior: same soft-threshold semantics, same preservation semantics, now running against pi's native compaction engine via the active Model.
80918049  - **Slash command seam:** `pi.registerCommand("compact", ...)` + `pi.registerCommand("clear", ...)` inside `createEmmyExtension` — right next to the existing `pi.registerShortcut("shift+ctrl+up/down", ...)` (Plan 03-08 pattern). Both commands take `ExtensionCommandContext` which has `.compact()`, `.newSession()`, `.waitForIdle()`, `.abort()`.
8cdc5659  - **SearxNG JSON API is plain JSON over HTTP GET.** No auth headers, no rate-limiting from localhost, no websockets. Request shape: `GET /search?q=<urlencoded>&format=json&safesearch=0`. Response: `{query, results: [{title, url, content, engine, ...}], answers, suggestions, ...}`. The new `web_search` tool can be ~60 lines of TS (fetch + decode + map) — smaller than web_fetch.
fd74a8b9  - **Badge state-machine is already module-level** (Plan 03-06). `setInitialAudit`, `flipToViolation`, `bindBadge`, `_lastResult` — Plan 03.1-02 extends the enum from `{green, red}` to `{green, yellow, red}` and adds `flipToYellow(reason)` / `flipToGreen(reason)` for SearxNG up/down transitions. ce630da1  <specifics>
5efbcb48  ## Specific Ideas
e3b0c442  
bf1820cf  - **Live-mode SC-2 3-variant matrix remains Phase 3 operator-gated (`p3-07 sc2 live green`).** Plan 03.1-01 resolves the wire-through blocker that was preventing live mode from even being attempted. After Plan 03.1-01 closes, the operator-gated item becomes UNBLOCKED but not CLOSED — closure still requires a ~2h GPU window. Plan 03.1-01 close walkthrough DOES include a live compaction firing (that's D-30's truth #7) but on a realistic single research session, not the full 200-turn synthetic SC-2 replay.
07d48375  - **SearxNG first-boot needs network.** Same as Langfuse + NGC vLLM. Plan 03.1-02 documents this: first pull of `searxng/searxng@sha256:...` requires connectivity. After that, `start_emmy.sh --airgap` is expected to work while SearxNG is running (because SearxNG's outbound connections go to Google/DDG/Brave/Bing UPSTREAMS — NOT to the SearxNG image registry).
334a6790  - **The "LOCAL LLM · WEB" badge wording matters.** Not "LOCAL LLM + WEB" (confusing — suggests web LLM); not "LOCAL INFERENCE · WEB SEARCH" (too long); not "LOCAL ONLY · SEARCH" (weak contrast). Middle-dot separator is the visual hint that inference is still local; web is an add-on.
93c27fdf  - **`/clear` is dangerous.** Unlike `/compact`, there's no undo. Plan 03.1-01 pins `ctx.ui.confirm("Clear session", "This will start a fresh session. Unsaved work in the transcript will remain on disk but the agent will lose all context. Continue?")` before `ctx.newSession()`. In non-interactive modes (`hasUI: false`), the command fails with a clear error message — `/clear` is interactive-only.
d8ce5a71  - **v3.1 is NOT v4.** The profile-version major bump is reserved for structural changes (new subsystem blocks, schema breaking changes). 0.88 → 0.55 is a tuning refinement. Adding `tools.web_search` block + `tools.web_fetch.search_bypass_ttl_ms` field is additive (schema stays backward-compat: Phase 3.1 pydantic patch declares them Optional, defaults to None/0). Hash changes → new version directory per D-02, but the minor-version naming preserves the "v3 → v3.1 → v4" intuition where v4 arrives with Gemma 4's arrival pattern change.
51042f58  - **Pitfall #3 is this phase's signature pitfall.** CLAUDE.md's exact quote: "Start at 0.75, validate with 30-min sustained load." Plan 03.1-01 effectively re-runs the Phase 1 KV-finder math with a stricter constraint: "highest value that doesn't preempt AND leaves ≥40GB system RAM free." The bisection isn't re-run; we set to 0.55 by conservative calculation and validate empirically with `free -h` + 10min warm session.
f2731592  - **Pitfall #6 still applies.** SP_OK canary fires on every session_start; `/clear` creates a new session; therefore `/clear` MUST NOT short-circuit the canary path. Plan 03.1-01 explicitly validates this in the walkthrough — after `/clear`, the boot banner should re-emit SP_OK within the first agent turn.
e3b0c442  
f6a17468  </specifics>
e3b0c442  
9ad1df08  <deferred>
99a3f2c5  ## Deferred Ideas (confirmed out-of-scope for Phase 3.1)
e3b0c442  
0550574a  - **Per-user SearxNG cookie settings** — SearxNG supports per-session UI preferences. Emmy is single-user and uses JSON API only; no cookies. Revisit only if we add a web UI.
c6999a1c  - **SearxNG engine weighting / category filters** — default engine priority (google > ddg > brave > bing) is hardcoded in settings.yml; emmy doesn't expose knobs. Revisit if certain search types (code, academic) need dedicated engines.
493dc0b7  - **Result scoring / de-duplication inside emmy** — SearxNG already de-duplicates cross-engine hits server-side. Emmy treats SearxNG's JSON output as authoritative.
10a56649  - **Cache warm-up / pre-fetch** — no; the agent's search pattern is unpredictable.
3f5170df  - **web_fetch bypass policy via domain bootstrap** — not needed; returned-URL bypass (D-35) covers the intended use case (agent searches, then fetches one of the returned results).
f7420c08  - **Manual `/search <query>` slash command** — redundant; the agent uses web_search as a tool call, and the user can ask the agent "search for X" via normal prompt.
7d8a0ef9  - **`/stop` or `/abort`** — pi 0.68 already handles Ctrl+C at the TUI layer; no need for a slash command duplicate.
aeeac4e7  - **Per-tool cost accounting** — lived-experience JSONL already captures `tool_calls` names; cost/metric rollup is Phase 5 eval territory.
0c901c50  - **`/profile v3` or `/profile v3.1` command** — Phase 4's `/profile` atomic-swap UX (UX-04) handles this. Phase 3.1 stays with "restart pi-emmy with a different `--profile` flag."
83f9568c  - **SearxNG WebUI for the operator** — port-bind 127.0.0.1:8888 makes the web UI reachable by browser for manual debug, but emmy doesn't document this as a supported path. Runbook mentions it briefly.
7e7dd96e  - **Compaction telemetry → Langfuse rich view** — existing `session.compaction.trigger/.complete/.fallback` events already flow through dual-sink to Langfuse; additional Langfuse-specific dashboards are Phase 7 polish.
e3b0c442  
5d8ff197  </deferred>

d65a67ca  # Phase 03.1: Operational Polish — Context
e3b0c442  
85381bba  **Gathered:** 2026-04-22
2d475642  **Status:** Ready for planning (discuss-phase skipped — urgent insertion)
5fcd5a30  **Insertion rationale:** Phase 3 closed 2026-04-22, but a single day of Phase-3-certified daily-driver use surfaced three operational defects that make the daily-driver bar unreachable in practice despite being formally "reached" at end of Phase 2. Phase 3.1 is inserted BEFORE Phase 4 (Gemma 4) because the Gemma 4 profile is contingent on a stable v3.1 harness footing.
e3b0c442  
a08c8e46  <domain>
2abaf200  ## Phase Boundary
e3b0c442  
73a459dd  Phase 3.1 ships **three surgical fixes + one new capability + daily-ops docs**:
e3b0c442  
92d5012f  **Fix A — RAM headroom (D-29):** `gpu_memory_utilization=0.88` on DGX Spark UMA (128 GB unified pool) reserves ~112 GB for GPU/KV, which steals from system RAM and produces 116/125 GB used + 8 GB swap during idle emmy-serve. CLAUDE.md Pitfall #3 explicitly warned ("Start at 0.75") but the Phase 1 KV-finder landed at 0.88 because the bisection objective was "highest value that doesn't preempt" rather than "highest value that leaves OS headroom". Drop to ~0.55 while KEEPING `max_model_len=131072` (user constraint: don't shrink context). Expected outcome: emmy-serve footprint ~50-70 GB, >40 GB system headroom, no swap pressure.
e3b0c442  
30753602  **Fix B — Live compaction + manual escape (D-30, D-31, D-32):** Plan 03-03 shipped `emmyCompactionTrigger` on `pi.on("turn_start")` with D-14 preservation + D-16 fallback + D-12 fail-loud. SC-2 green in stub mode. But the trigger's `engine.summarize()` injection point was never wired to pi's native `ctx.compact(options)` method (verified at `dist/core/extensions/types.d.ts:224`). Result: agent hit the 114,688-token hard wall during a real research session; compaction never fired; no manual escape (`/clear` attempted as a pi slash command failed — `/clear` is not a pi 0.68 built-in, and at 114,689 tokens the session couldn't process any further input). Plan 03.1-01 wires the turn_start handler to call `ctx.compact({customInstructions})` directly and adds two pi slash commands: `/compact [optional instructions]` and `/clear`.
e3b0c442  
997f2de5  **Fix C — Local research egress (D-33, D-34, D-35, D-36):** v3 profile ships a 5-host doc allowlist (docs.python.org, developer.mozilla.org, docs.vllm.ai, huggingface.co, docs.langfuse.com). Too narrow for a daily-driver research loop. The thesis is "NO CLOUD INFERENCE" — the LLM always runs locally on emmy-serve. "NO OUTBOUND TRAFFIC AT ALL" was a broader interpretation that conflates the two. Phase 3.1 refines the thesis: local LLM is non-negotiable; local-first EGRESS (a single self-hosted SearxNG sibling to Langfuse) is acceptable for web research. SearxNG is self-hosted at `127.0.0.1:8888`, auditable, and the harness only talks to SearxNG (which in turn talks to Google + DDG + Brave + Bing upstreams). New `web_search` tool hits the loopback SearxNG. `web_fetch` gets returned-URL bypass semantics: any URL returned by `web_search` in the last N seconds/results is fetchable without allowlist entry. Badge semantics split into three states: `OFFLINE OK` (green) when SearxNG is down/disabled; `LOCAL LLM · WEB` (yellow) when SearxNG is up and web_search is live; `CLOUD INFERENCE` (red) reserved for actual thesis violation (non-loopback inference detected — never fires today).
e3b0c442  
6f0e448f  **New artifact — Profile v3.1 (D-37):** Sibling of v3 per D-02 immutability. v3 stays byte-identical. v3.1 = v3 + (a) tuned `serving.yaml` with `gpu_memory_utilization: 0.55`, `max_num_batched_tokens: 8192`; (b) updated `harness.yaml` with `tools.web_search` block + `tools.web_fetch` bypass policy (allowlist retained for backwards compat); (c) `PROFILE_NOTES.md` provenance for D-29..D-36; (d) new prompts nothing — compaction prompts unchanged from v3.
e3b0c442  
e6d6f8c8  **New docs (D-38 sequencing, Plan 03.1-03):** Top-level `README.md` for the project (prerequisites + quickstart + stack boot/teardown + profile switching + troubleshooting); `docs/runbook.md` for daily ops; `CLAUDE.md` revised to split "No cloud INFERENCE" (hard invariant, air-gap tested) from "Local-first EGRESS" (SearxNG is the only outbound component, auditable, loopback-only from harness's POV).
e3b0c442  
33f3739a  **Out of Phase 3.1 (deferred):**
e917979c  - Gemma 4 profile (Phase 4 — now depends on 3.1 instead of 3)
e06de658  - `/profile` atomic swap UX (Phase 4)
b537ac13  - Eval harness (Phase 5) — `/compact` / `/clear` commands DO NOT trigger regression runs; they are daily-ops primitives
b5bf0006  - SearxNG engine cache expiry tuning — use upstream defaults; revisit if rate-limit whack-a-mole surfaces
13d988d9  - Per-search quota budgets — not worth planning before lived-experience data accumulates
bfc30694  - Speculative decoding (Phase 6)
d040302e  - Public artifact polish (Phase 7) — Phase 3.1 docs are daily-ops, not publication
e3b0c442  
c242daee  </domain>
e3b0c442  
7570c21a  <decisions>
84013090  ## Implementation Decisions (Locked)
e3b0c442  
efe339e5  **Locked by user at phase insertion time 2026-04-22. Carry NON-NEGOTIABLE status equivalent to Phase 3's D-01..D-28. None contradict prior decisions — each is a refinement or a net-new capability.**
e3b0c442  
94b8c472  ### D-29 — RAM target
d8de2c5c  Drop `serving.yaml.engine.gpu_memory_utilization` from 0.88 → 0.55 in v3.1. KEEP `max_model_len=131072` (user constraint: don't reduce model capability). Drop `max_num_batched_tokens` from 16384 → 8192 (still well-above the "interactive latency" threshold from STACK.md; reduces CUDA workspace footprint). Target emmy-serve footprint ~50-70 GB, leaving >40 GB system headroom. Post-deploy validation: `free -h` MUST show `available ≥ 40G` after 10 min of warm emmy-serve + Langfuse compose. v3 profile is NOT modified.
e3b0c442  
c1251dfa  ### D-30 — Live compaction actually fires
785f8d66  `emmyCompactionTrigger` at `packages/emmy-context/src/compaction.ts` already contains D-11 soft-threshold check, D-14 preservation pre-filter, D-12 hard-ceiling, D-16 fallback. The `pi-emmy-extension.ts` `pi.on("turn_start", ...)` handler invokes the trigger but today's `engine.summarize()` default throws ("not configured"). D-30 locks the live wiring: the turn_start handler MUST call `ctx.compact({customInstructions})` directly (pi 0.68 ExtensionContext.compact method, verified at `dist/core/extensions/types.d.ts:224`). Emmy retains its preservation classifier (runs BEFORE the ctx.compact call to annotate which entries are preservation-critical), but the actual summarization round-trip flows through pi's native compaction engine — which already reuses the active Model from the session (the same emmy-vllm endpoint). Preservation-policy enforcement happens by passing `customInstructions` that include emmy's D-14 preservation directive text (from `prompts/compact.md`).
e3b0c442  
50f0953f  ### D-31 — /compact slash command
161f0697  Add `pi.registerCommand("compact", { handler })` in `packages/emmy-ux/src/pi-emmy-extension.ts`. Handler signature: `async (args, ctx) => ctx.compact({ customInstructions: args || undefined })`. Manual trigger; user can force compaction at any moment (useful when running many file reads before asking for a summary). Works even below the D-11 soft threshold.
e3b0c442  
c9e527e5  ### D-32 — /clear slash command
567a7bd8  Add `pi.registerCommand("clear", { handler })` in `packages/emmy-ux/src/pi-emmy-extension.ts`. Handler uses `ctx.newSession({})` from pi 0.68 `ExtensionCommandContext` (verified at `dist/core/extensions/types.d.ts:236-241`). Emmy's SP_OK canary + 3-layer prompt assembly re-run automatically on the new session because session.ts's boot hooks fire on each AgentSession creation. `/clear` is the only escape from an over-full session state short of killing pi-emmy.
e3b0c442  
a67690dd  ### D-33 — SearxNG topology
dff64743  Self-hosted SearxNG Docker Compose stack at `observability/searxng/docker-compose.yaml`, sibling of `observability/langfuse/docker-compose.yaml`. Single service (plus Redis for the search cache — use the SAME `redis` container Langfuse already runs via `external: true`, no new Redis). Digest-pinned image per D-09 (Phase 3) air-gap discipline. Bind `127.0.0.1:8888:8080` loopback-only. Config `observability/searxng/settings.yml` enables these engines:
7fca3f4c  - `google` (primary, preferred)
18d5e699  - `duckduckgo`, `brave`, `bing` (automatic fallbacks — SearxNG rotates on rate-limit or timeout, which is the exact problem we're solving)
e3b0c442  
b2370b0e  Setup script `scripts/start_searxng.sh` mirrors `start_observability.sh`: openssl-generated secret-key, health-gate with 60s timeout, instructions banner at end. `scripts/stop_searxng.sh` mirrors `stop_observability.sh` with optional `--volumes` flag.
e3b0c442  
d60f9776  ### D-34 — web_search tool
4ed5fcd0  New tool at `packages/emmy-tools/src/web-search.ts`:
f1b90184  ```
ba52ea45  web_search(query: string, max_results?: number = 10): Promise<SearchResult[]>
f1b90184  ```
2ae75dd2  Where `SearchResult = { title: string; url: string; snippet: string; engine: string }`. Implementation: HTTP GET to `http://127.0.0.1:8888/search?q=<urlencoded>&format=json&safesearch=0&max_results=<n>`. On HTTP 5xx or timeout (10s default), returns empty array + emits `tool.web_search.fallback` telemetry event; does NOT throw (pi agent continues). On kill-switch (EMMY_WEB_SEARCH=off OR EMMY_TELEMETRY=off), the tool is unregistered — pi never advertises it to the model.
e3b0c442  
0d166580  ### D-35 — web_fetch returned-URL bypass
18539d8c  `packages/emmy-tools/src/web-fetch-allowlist.ts` grows a sibling concept to the hostname allowlist: a rolling in-memory `recentSearchUrls: Set<string>` populated by `web_search` on every successful call. URL is fetchable IFF `hostname ∈ allowlist` OR `url ∈ recentSearchUrls` (exact URL match, not hostname). Entries expire after 5 minutes (configurable via `tools.web_fetch.search_bypass_ttl_ms` in harness.yaml, default 300000). Plan 03.1-02 also preserves v3.1's hostname allowlist with the same 5 default hosts as v3, so existing allowlisted doc-host flows continue to work.
e3b0c442  
6cba49e2  ### D-36 — Badge rename (three-state)
65120ce6  `packages/emmy-ux/src/offline-badge.ts` grows from two states to three:
23374c84  - `[emmy] OFFLINE OK` (green) — SearxNG is down, disabled, or unreachable; web_search unavailable; fully air-gapped posture
f682467e  - `[emmy] LOCAL LLM · WEB` (yellow, new ANSI 33) — SearxNG is up and healthy; web_search live; LLM inference still local-only
0be0c140  - `[emmy] CLOUD INFERENCE` (red) — reserved for actual thesis violation; non-loopback inference endpoint detected
e3b0c442  
bd122858  The existing "NETWORK USED" label is DEPRECATED; boot-time tool audit now distinguishes benign egress (SearxNG) from thesis-breaking egress (non-loopback vLLM/inference). The red state should NEVER fire with current code — it's structurally reserved so that Phase 4 (when multi-model routing arrives) and Phase 6 (speculative decoding proxies) have a natural home for detection.
e3b0c442  
67425942  ### D-37 — Profile v3.1
69b6df2e  New sibling directory `profiles/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3.1/`. v3 stays byte-identical (D-02 immutability). v3.1 bundle:
0789b2cb  - `serving.yaml`: copy of v3 with `gpu_memory_utilization: 0.55`, `max_num_batched_tokens: 8192` (D-29)
37e055be  - `harness.yaml`: copy of v3 with `tools.web_search` block added + `tools.web_fetch` block extended with `search_bypass_ttl_ms: 300000` (allowlist retained from v3; default hosts unchanged)
4f1ad956  - `prompts/`: copy of v3's prompts verbatim (compaction prompts unchanged)
ae7bb933  - `grammars/`: copy of v3's grammars verbatim
cbd5daa7  - `tool_schemas/`: copy of v3's schemas + new `web_search.schema.json`
e01ff47d  - `PROFILE_NOTES.md`: new with provenance for D-29..D-36; validation_runs appended after Plan 03.1-01 close walkthrough
d68dc9f7  - `profile.yaml`: new; hash computed post-authoring via `uv run emmy profile hash --write`
e3b0c442  
5e98aa2b  Profile validate (`uv run emmy profile validate`) MUST exit 0 against v1, v2, v3, AND v3.1 at every plan close (four-way regression gate extended). `start_emmy.sh` default profile bumps v3 → v3.1 in Plan 03.1-03 (day-to-day-docs plan — the bump is an end-of-phase operator-affecting change that belongs with the README).
e3b0c442  
96eb2e4c  ### D-38 — Sequential wave execution
08563378  Plans 03.1-01 → 03.1-02 → 03.1-03 run in waves 1, 2, 3. Do NOT parallelize. Rationale:
bc45e5b7  - 03.1-01 unblocks daily-driver use (fixes the compaction wall). Must close first.
9435a4e6  - 03.1-02 introduces SearxNG + new tool + badge refactor. Must wait for 03.1-01 because it modifies `pi-emmy-extension.ts` at the same seam 03.1-01 touches (slash-command registration + session_start hook).
59667933  - 03.1-03 documents the final state. Must wait for 03.1-01 AND 03.1-02 to close because it references the v3.1 profile as the default in `start_emmy.sh` + README.
e3b0c442  
7864b664  ## Claude's Discretion (additional, no conflict with locked decisions)
e3b0c442  
91574675  - Exact choice between ANSI yellow (`\x1b[33m`) and ANSI yellow-bold for the new `LOCAL LLM · WEB` badge state — planner picks based on terminal palette; the literal text `LOCAL LLM · WEB` is locked.
60e3b23b  - Whether SearxNG's settings.yml lives in `observability/searxng/settings.yml` mounted read-only into the container or is generated at `start_searxng.sh` time from a committed `settings.template.yml`. Pattern: langfuse uses committed + env-var-injected secrets; SearxNG has no secrets except the `server.secret_key` which is analogous. Planner picks the template+generator pattern for consistency.
517a9f2e  - Slash command `/compact` argument handling: whether the argument becomes a whole prompt override or an addendum to `prompts/compact.md`. Locked: addendum. The profile-defined preservation policy MUST apply regardless of user-supplied instructions; `args` only adds focus hints ("focus on the search results, the file reads can be dropped").
c62232c8  - Slash command `/clear` behavior around the running agent: whether to call `ctx.abort()` + `ctx.waitForIdle()` before `ctx.newSession()` — yes. The command handler receives `ExtensionCommandContext` which exposes both. Planner pins this ordering in the plan.
3e17b634  - Badge transition timing on SearxNG health change: whether the badge re-polls every N seconds or is event-driven. Planner's call. Event-driven on every web_search success/failure is simpler and matches the pattern from UX-03 (runtime enforcement re-renders on every web_fetch).
fee535e3  - `web_search` fallback behavior when SearxNG is completely unreachable: empty-array return (silently useless) vs. ToolError-shaped failure (pi surfaces error to agent). Locked: ToolError-shaped — the agent needs to know web_search is unavailable so it can try other approaches (e.g., web_fetch against allowlisted docs directly).
5c175f5b  - SearxNG Docker image choice — SearxNG upstream at `searxng/searxng:latest` is actively maintained; prior forks (`searx/searx`) are abandoned. Digest-pin the current latest as of plan time.
e3b0c442  
4b95d434  </decisions>
e3b0c442

2976bc93  ## Existing Code Insights
e3b0c442  
b3f26250  - **`emmyCompactionTrigger` injection seam is already correct.** Plan 03-03 left `engine.summarize` as an injectable function that throws "not configured" by default. Plan 03.1-01 replaces that with the live wiring — NOT by implementing a raw HTTP round-trip, but by having the turn_start handler in `pi-emmy-extension.ts` call `ctx.compact({customInstructions})` DIRECTLY. This is a cleaner architecture than injecting a summarize function because pi's ctx.compact already knows about the active Model and apiKey. The `emmyCompactionTrigger` body keeps its role as the D-14 preservation classifier + D-11 soft-threshold + D-12 hard-ceiling guardrails; when the trigger determines compaction SHOULD fire, it now emits a structured directive (a preparation object) rather than calling summarize itself, and the turn_start handler reads the directive + calls ctx.compact. Net behavior: same soft-threshold semantics, same preservation semantics, now running against pi's native compaction engine via the active Model.
80918049  - **Slash command seam:** `pi.registerCommand("compact", ...)` + `pi.registerCommand("clear", ...)` inside `createEmmyExtension` — right next to the existing `pi.registerShortcut("shift+ctrl+up/down", ...)` (Plan 03-08 pattern). Both commands take `ExtensionCommandContext` which has `.compact()`, `.newSession()`, `.waitForIdle()`, `.abort()`.
8cdc5659  - **SearxNG JSON API is plain JSON over HTTP GET.** No auth headers, no rate-limiting from localhost, no websockets. Request shape: `GET /search?q=<urlencoded>&format=json&safesearch=0`. Response: `{query, results: [{title, url, content, engine, ...}], answers, suggestions, ...}`. The new `web_search` tool can be ~60 lines of TS (fetch + decode + map) — smaller than web_fetch.
fd74a8b9  - **Badge state-machine is already module-level** (Plan 03-06). `setInitialAudit`, `flipToViolation`, `bindBadge`, `_lastResult` — Plan 03.1-02 extends the enum from `{green, red}` to `{green, yellow, red}` and adds `flipToYellow(reason)` / `flipToGreen(reason)` for SearxNG up/down transitions. The existing `flipToViolation` stays reserved for the D-36 red state (non-loopback inference).
a3a95852  - **Profile hash `--write`** (Phase 2 Plan 02-07 pattern) — `uv run emmy profile hash --write profiles/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3.1` computes + writes to `profile.yaml`. Strips any `KNOWN-STALE` comments. v3 stays byte-identical (NOT modified).
a1a3c2b2  - **Four-way regression** (Phase 2+3 pattern): `bun test` + `bun run typecheck` + `uv run pytest tests/unit -q` + `uv run emmy profile validate v1+v2+v3+v3.1`. Any plan close that doesn't green all four is not a close. Plan 03.1-01 adds v3.1 to the validate sweep.
13f6e6ae  - **Air-gap CI split** (Plan 03.1-02): `emmy_serve/airgap/ci_verify_phase3.py` asserts ZERO outbound from emmy-serve + Langfuse. Plan 03.1-02 clones it to `ci_verify_research_egress.py` which permits `searxng.` outbound (container DNS name) but STILL blocks `api.openai.com`, `api.anthropic.com`, `*.cloudflare.com`, etc. Both validators live side-by-side; existing Phase 3 CI job continues to exist for the "strict loopback" gate while a new Phase 3.1 job covers "research egress permitted".

5efbcb48  ## Specific Ideas
e3b0c442  
bf1820cf  - **Live-mode SC-2 3-variant matrix remains Phase 3 operator-gated (`p3-07 sc2 live green`).** Plan 03.1-01 resolves the wire-through blocker that was preventing live mode from even being attempted. After Plan 03.1-01 closes, the operator-gated item becomes UNBLOCKED but not CLOSED — closure still requires a ~2h GPU window. Plan 03.1-01 close walkthrough DOES include a live compaction firing (that's D-30's truth #7) but on a realistic single research session, not the full 200-turn synthetic SC-2 replay.
07d48375  - **SearxNG first-boot needs network.** Same as Langfuse + NGC vLLM. Plan 03.1-02 documents this: first pull of `searxng/searxng@sha256:...` requires connectivity. After that, `start_emmy.sh --airgap` is expected to work while SearxNG is running (because SearxNG's outbound connections go to Google/DDG/Brave/Bing UPSTREAMS — NOT to the SearxNG image registry).
334a6790  - **The "LOCAL LLM · WEB" badge wording matters.** Not "LOCAL LLM + WEB" (confusing — suggests web LLM); not "LOCAL INFERENCE · WEB SEARCH" (too long); not "LOCAL ONLY · SEARCH" (weak contrast). Middle-dot separator is the visual hint that inference is still local; web is an add-on.
93c27fdf  - **`/clear` is dangerous.** Unlike `/compact`, there's no undo. Plan 03.1-01 pins `ctx.ui.confirm("Clear session", "This will start a fresh session. Unsaved work in the transcript will remain on disk but the agent will lose all context. Continue?")` before `ctx.newSession()`. In non-interactive modes (`hasUI: false`), the command fails with a clear error message — `/clear` is interactive-only.
d8ce5a71  - **v3.1 is NOT v4.** The profile-version major bump is reserved for structural changes (new subsystem blocks, schema breaking changes). 0.88 → 0.55 is a tuning refinement. Adding `tools.web_search` block + `tools.web_fetch.search_bypass_ttl_ms` field is additive (schema stays backward-compat: Phase 3.1 pydantic patch declares them Optional, defaults to None/0). Hash changes → new version directory per D-02, but the minor-version naming preserves the "v3 → v3.1 → v4" intuition where v4 arrives with Gemma 4's arrival pattern change.
51042f58  - **Pitfall #3 is this phase's signature pitfall.** CLAUDE.md's exact quote: "Start at 0.75, validate with 30-min sustained load." Plan 03.1-01 effectively re-runs the Phase 1 KV-finder math with a stricter constraint: "highest value that doesn't preempt AND leaves ≥40GB system RAM free." The bisection isn't re-run; we set to 0.55 by conservative calculation and validate empirically with `free -h` + 10min warm session.
f2731592  - **Pitfall #6 still applies.** SP_OK canary fires on every session_start; `/clear` creates a new session; therefore `/clear` MUST NOT short-circuit the canary path. Plan 03.1-01 explicitly validates this in the walkthrough — after `/clear`, the boot banner should re-emit SP_OK within the first agent turn.

99a3f2c5  ## Deferred Ideas (confirmed out-of-scope for Phase 3.1)
e3b0c442  
0550574a  - **Per-user SearxNG cookie settings** — SearxNG supports per-session UI preferences. Emmy is single-user and uses JSON API only; no cookies. Revisit only if we add a web UI.
c6999a1c  - **SearxNG engine weighting / category filters** — default engine priority (google > ddg > brave > bing) is hardcoded in settings.yml; emmy doesn't expose knobs. Revisit if certain search types (code, academic) need dedicated engines.
493dc0b7  - **Result scoring / de-duplication inside emmy** — SearxNG already de-duplicates cross-engine hits server-side. Emmy treats SearxNG's JSON output as authoritative.
10a56649  - **Cache warm-up / pre-fetch** — no; the agent's search pattern is unpredictable.
3f5170df  - **web_fetch bypass policy via domain bootstrap** — not needed; returned-URL bypass (D-35) covers the intended use case (agent searches, then fetches one of the returned results).
f7420c08  - **Manual `/search <query>` slash command** — redundant; the agent uses web_search as a tool call, and the user can ask the agent "search for X" via normal prompt.
7d8a0ef9  - **`/stop` or `/abort`** — pi 0.68 already handles Ctrl+C at the TUI layer; no need for a slash command duplicate.
aeeac4e7  - **Per-tool cost accounting** — lived-experience JSONL already captures `tool_calls` names; cost/metric rollup is Phase 5 eval territory.
0c901c50  - **`/profile v3` or `/profile v3.1` command** — Phase 4's `/profile` atomic-swap UX (UX-04) handles this. Phase 3.1 stays with "restart pi-emmy with a different `--profile` flag."
83f9568c  - **SearxNG WebUI for the operator** — port-bind 127.0.0.1:8888 makes the web UI reachable by browser for manual debug, but emmy doesn't document this as a supported path. Runbook mentions it briefly.
7e7dd96e  - **Compaction telemetry → Langfuse rich view** — existing `session.compaction.trigger/.complete/.fallback` events already flow through dual-sink to Langfuse; additional Langfuse-specific dashboards are Phase 7 polish.

content.text:
The v3.1 profile's `gpu_memory_utilization` value is **0.55**.

**Why it was picked:**

The prior v3 profile had `gpu_memory_utilization: 0.88`, which was determined by the Phase 1 KV-finder's automated bisection (`scripts/find_kv_budget.py`). That bisection's objective was "highest value that doesn't cause preemption" — and at 0.88, the engine achieved zero preemptions across 10 sustained-load iterations. However, on the DGX Spark's 128 GB **UMA** (unified memory architecture), this left only ~12 GB for system RAM, resulting in 116/125 GB used + 8 GB swap during idle emmy-serve operation.

The Phase 3.1 team (designated **D-29**) dropped it to **0.55** to free system RAM headroom. The rationale:

1. **CLAUDE.md Pitfall #3** had explicitly warned: "DGX Spark UMA shares model + KV + harness CPU. Default `gpu_memory_utilization=0.95` causes preemption. Start at 0.75, validate with 30-min sustained load." The Phase 1 KV-finder had optimized for "no preempt" without accounting for OS-side RAM headroom.

2. The target was to bring emmy-serve's footprint down to ~50-70 GB, leaving **>40 GB of system RAM free** (no swap pressure).

3. `max_model_len` was kept at 131072 (128K context) — a user constraint that the context size must not be reduced.

4. `max_num_batched_tokens` was also dropped from 16384 → 8192 to further reduce the CUDA workspace footprint.

Post-deploy validation confirmed `free -h` showed ~42 GiB available (9× the ~4.6 GiB from v3), with no swap pressure.
